zh.title: 股权转让责任变革：新旧公司法下原股东责任边界重构
zh.lead: 随着新《公司法》于2024年7月1日正式实施，关于股东未届出资期限转让股权后的责任承担问题成为实务焦点。本文通过对比新旧法律体系，结合最高院最新司法解释，系统梳理原股东在公司债务中的责任边界，为债权人、股东提供维权的参考路径。

blocks.text: 新旧公司法责任体系对比解析
原《公司法》及司法解释的规制逻辑

blocks.title: 《最高人民法院关于适用〈中华人民共和国公司法〉若干问题的规定（三）》
blocks.content:
第十八条　有限责任公司的股东未履行或者未全面履行出资义务即转让股权，受让人对此知道或者应当知道，公司请求该股东履行出资义务、受让人对此承担连带责任的，人民法院应予支持；公司债权人依照本规定第十三条第二款向该股东提起诉讼，同时请求前述受让人对此承担连带责任的，人民法院应予支持。
受让人根据前款规定承担责任后，向该未履行或者未全面履行出资义务的股东追偿的，人民法院应予支持。但是，当事人另有约定的除外。
该解释适用的前提是股东在未履行或未全面履行出资义务的情况下转让股权，受让人如果知道或应当知道原股东存在出资瑕疵，需对公司或债权人承担连带责任。同时该解释赋予了受让人追偿权，受让人承担责任后，有权向原股东追偿，除非双方另有约定。债权人或公司需提供证据证明受让人“知道或应当知道”原股东的出资瑕疵，否则难以要求受让人承担责任。实践中，法院会根据转让方与受让方之间的关系（如亲属关系、职务关系）、股权转让对价是否合理等因素推定受让人的知情状态。

blocks.text: 新《公司法》第八十八条的制度创新
《中华人民共和国公司法》
第八十八条　股东转让已认缴出资但未届出资期限的股权的，由受让人承担缴纳该出资的义务；受让人未按期足额缴纳出资的，转让人对受让人未按期缴纳的出资承担补充责任。
未按照公司章程规定的出资日期缴纳出资或者作为出资的非货币财产的实际价额显著低于所认缴的出资额的股东转让股权的，转让人与受让人在出资不足的范围内承担连带责任；受让人不知道且不应当知道存在上述情形的，由转让人承担责任。

blocks.title: 《公司法》第八十八条第一款的立法依据在于债务加入，未出资股权转让后，原股东仍要对受让人未按期缴纳的出资承担补充责任。
blocks.content: 第八十八条第二款是关于瑕疵股权转让的出资责任分配，在此情形下转让人在受让人出资不足范围内承担连带责任，受让人善意的，由转让人担责。但是债权人提供反驳证据，证明受让人实际知道或者应当知道存在出资瑕疵的，则受让人不免责，比如说受让价格明显异常，远低于正常股权转让价格，出让人对此没有合理解释的。

blocks.text: 原股东担责的适用场景
（一）2024年7月1日之前股东未届出资期限转让股权引发的出资责任纠纷

blocks.title: 观点一：原股东不免责，仍有出资责任
blocks.content: 该观点认为虽然未届认缴出资期限的股权转让属于双方之合意，其符合债权债务的概括移转的形式要求，但是股权转让涉及到公司的资本充实，股东对公司的出资承诺及补足系公司法强制性规定，股权转让的交易自由和股东期限利益的行使不能损害公司和公司债权人的利益，原股东的股本认缴不能随着股权移转，原股东仍需承担出资责任。
(2021)苏03民终7173号案，二审法院认为虽然在股权转让时出资期限尚未届满，但《公司法司法解释三》第十八条中“有限责任公司的股东未履行或者未全面履行出资义务即转让股权”未对出资期限届满前及届满后转让股权进行区分。故而不论在出资期间届满前还是届满后转让股权，均落于第十八条的规范意旨中，原股东应当履行出资义务。

blocks.title: 观点二：原股东不再承担责任

blocks.content: (2021)皖民终427号案，一审法院以《公司法司法解释三》第十三条第三款为裁判依据，判定原股东承担补充赔偿责任。但二审法院认为原股东承担责任的前提是该股东在公司设立时即负有实际缴纳出资的义务，而股东在出资期间届满前转让股权的，相应的出资义务应当视为一并转移，原股东不应再对受让人的出资义务承担责任。

blocks.title: 观点三：原则上原股东不再承担责任，但是能证明原股东恶意侵害债权人利益的除外

blocks.content: （2024）粤19民终853号民事判决中指出，本案股东转让未届出资期限股权时，公司处于正常经营状况，虽然负有债务，但没有证据证明股权转让时公司存在不能清偿到期债务的情形，且受让人也不存在明显缺乏缴纳出资义务能力的情形，该股权转让属正常商业行为。因此，法院未认定股东转让未届出资期限股权时具有逃避出资义务的恶意进而未判令其承担责任。
而（2020）京03民终3634号案中股东转让股权时虽然未届出资期限，但转让时股东明知公司不能清偿到期债务，且受让人明显缺乏缴纳出资能力。此种股权转让增加公司注册资本实缴到位的风险，影响公司债权人到期债权的实现，显然属于以股权转让方式恶意逃避出资义务的情形，转让人依法应当承担出资责任。

blocks.text: （二）2024年7月1日之后股东未届出资期限转让股权引发的出资责任纠纷
1.必须承担责任的法定情形

blocks.content: 股东转让未届出资期限的股权，当受让人未按期足额缴纳出资，原股东需对未缴纳部分承担补充责任，但不包含利息。在（2024）内04民终5215号案中，二审法院认为，债权人与公司的债务发生于原股东任公司股东期间，原股东未足额履行出资义务即转让股权，在公司不能清偿债务并执行终本的情况下，法院依申请追加原股东为被执行人符合法律规定。

blocks.text: 若原股东未按章程缴纳出资或非货币资产显著不足，即便股权已转让，仍与受让人在出资不足范围内连带担责。
2.免责抗辩的可行路径
原股东若能证明出资已实缴或不存在瑕疵，或者债权形成于股权转让之后，基于信赖利益保护，原股东可以主张免责。此外，若债权人主张债务时已过诉讼时效，原股东可通过主张时效抗辩来免除责任。
债权人利益保护路径优化建议
诉讼阶段
穿透式追索
同时起诉原股东与受让人（尤其在出资不实情形）
善用《企业破产法》第三十五条加速到期规则
证据收集要点
调取工商内档核查出资承诺
获取股权转让价款支付凭证
收集原股东抽逃出资证据链
（二）执行阶段
《关于民事执行中变更、追加当事人若干问题的规定》
第十九条　作为被执行人的公司，财产不足以清偿生效法律文书确定的债务，其股东未依法履行出资义务即转让股权，申请执行人申请变更、追加该原股东或依公司法规定对该出资承担连带责任的发起人为被执行人，在未依法出资的范围内承担责任的，人民法院应予支持。
变更或者追加原股东为被执行人，使其在未依法出资的范围内承担责任。

references: 《中华人民共和国企业破产法》
《最高人民法院关于适用〈中华人民共和国公司法〉若干问题的规定（三）》（2020年修正）
《关于民事执行中变更、追加当事人若干问题的规定》
《最高人民法院关于适用〈中华人民共和国公司法〉时间效力的若干规定》
zh.subtitle: ——《公司法》第八十八条
zh.conclusion: 新《公司法》下未届期股权转让责任规则的变革，对各方主体都提出了新要求。无论是原股东、受让人还是债权人，都需准确把握规则变化，合理规划商事行为，防范法律风险。若您在实务中遇到相关问题，欢迎联系我们，北京青颂律师事务所将为您提供专业的法律服务。

en.title: Reform of Equity Transfer Responsibility: Reconstruction of the Original Shareholders' Liability Boundary under the Old and New Company Laws
en.lead: With the formal implementation of the new Company Law on July 1, 2024, the issue of liability for shareholders after transferring their equity within the term of capital contribution has become the focus of practice. By comparing the old and new legal systems and combining the latest judicial interpretations of the Supreme People's Court, this article systematically combs the liability boundaries of the original shareholders in corporate debts, and provides a reference path for creditors and shareholders to safeguard their rights.

blocks.text: Comparative Analysis of the Liability System of the New and Old Company Laws
The regulatory logic of the original Company Law and judicial interpretations

blocks.title: Provisions of the Supreme People's Court on Several Issues Concerning the Application of the Company Law of the People's Republic of China (III)
blocks.content:
Article 18 Where a shareholder of a limited liability company transfers his equity without performing or fully performing his obligation of capital contribution, and the transferee knows or should know about it, and the company requests the shareholder to perform his obligation of capital contribution, and the transferee shall bear joint and several liability for it, the people's court shall support it;Where a creditor of the company brings a lawsuit against the shareholder in accordance with the second paragraph of Article 13 of these Provisions and requests the above-mentioned transferee to bear joint and several liability for this, the people's court shall support it.
 If the transferee, after assuming the liability in accordance with the provisions of the preceding paragraph, claims compensation from the shareholder who has not fulfilled or has not fully fulfilled the obligation of capital contribution, the people's court shall support it.However, unless otherwise agreed upon by the parties.
 The premise for the application of this interpretation is that the shareholder transfers the equity without fulfilling or fully fulfilling the obligation of capital contribution, and the transferee shall be jointly and severally liable to the company or creditors if he knows or should know that the original shareholder has a defect in capital contribution.At the same time, the interpretation gives the transferee the right of recourse, and the transferee has the right to recover from the original shareholder after assuming the responsibility, unless otherwise agreed by both parties.The creditor or the company shall provide evidence to prove that the transferee "knows or should know" the defect of the original shareholder's capital contribution, otherwise it is difficult to require the transferee to assume responsibility.In practice, the court will presume the informed state of the transferee according to the relationship between the transferor and the transferee, the reasonableness of the equity transfer price and other factors.

blocks.text: Institutional Innovation in Article 88 of the New Company Law
Company Law of the People's Republic of China

Article 88 Where a shareholder transfers the equity that has subscribed for the capital contribution but has not yet reached the time limit for capital contribution, the transferee shall bear the obligation to pay the capital contribution;If the transferee fails to pay the capital contribution in full and on time, the transferor shall bear supplementary liability for the capital contribution that the transferee fails to pay on time.

Where a shareholder who fails to pay the capital contribution on the date of capital contribution stipulated in the articles of association or whose actual value of the non-monetary property as capital contribution is significantly lower than the subscribed capital contribution transfers its equity, the transferor and the transferee shall be jointly and severally liable within the scope of insufficient capital contribution;If the transferee does not know and should not know that the above circumstances exist, the transferor shall be liable.

blocks.title: The legislative basis of the first paragraph of Article 88 of the Company Law is the addition of debts. After the transfer of the equity that has not been contributed, the original shareholder shall still bear supplementary liability for the capital contribution that the transferee has not paid on time.
blocks.content: Paragraph 2 of Article 88 refers to the distribution of capital contribution liability for the transfer of defective equity. Under such circumstances, the transferor shall bear joint and several liability within the scope of insufficient capital contribution of the transferee, and the transferor shall be liable if the transferee is in good faith.However, if the creditor provides rebuttal evidence to prove that the transferee actually knows or should know that there is a defect in the capital contribution, the transferee shall not be exempted from liability, for example, the transfer price is obviously abnormal, far lower than the normal equity transfer price, and the transferor has no reasonable explanation for this.

blocks.text: Applicable scenarios for the responsibility of the original shareholders
(1) Disputes over capital contribution liability arising from the transfer of equity by shareholders before the expiration of the capital contribution period before July 1, 2024

blocks.title: Opinion 1: The original shareholder is not exempt from liability and still has the responsibility for capital contribution
blocks.content: This view holds that although the transfer of equity within the term of subscribed capital contribution is a mutual agreement between the two parties, which conforms to the formal requirements of the general assignment of creditor's rights and debts, the transfer of equity involves the capital adequacy of the company. The shareholder's commitment to and obligation to make up capital contributions are mandatory provisions of the Company Law. The freedom of equity transfer transactions and the exercise of shareholders' term interests shall not harm the interests of the company and the company's creditors. The original shareholder's subscribed capital cannot be transferred along with the equity, and the original shareholder still needs to bear the responsibility for capital contribution.

(2021) SU03 Min Zhong No. 7173 Case, the court of second instance considers that although the time limit for capital contribution has not expired at the time of equity transfer, Article 18 of Judicial Interpretation III of the Company Law stipulates that "the shareholders of a limited liability company transfer their equity before and after the expiration of the time limit for capital contribution if they fail to perform or fully perform their obligations for capital contribution" does not distinguish between the transfer of equity before and after the expiration of the time limit for capital contribution. Therefore, whether the equity is transferred before or after the expiration of the capital contribution period, it falls within the normative intent of Article 18, and the original shareholder shall perform the obligation of capital contribution.

blocks.title: Point 2: The original shareholder is no longer liable

blocks.content: (2021) Wan Min Zhong No. 427 Case, the court of first instance took the third paragraph of Article 13 of Judicial Interpretation III of the Company Law as the judgment basis, and determined that the original shareholder shall be liable for supplementary compensation. However, the court of second instance considers that the premise of the original shareholder's liability is that the shareholder has the obligation to actually pay the capital contribution at the time of the establishment of the company, and if the shareholder transfers the equity before the expiration of the capital contribution period, the corresponding capital contribution obligations shall be deemed to be transferred together, and the original shareholder shall no longer be liable for the transferee's capital contribution obligations.

blocks.title: Opinion 3: In principle, the original shareholder shall no longer be liable, except that it can be proved that the original shareholder maliciously infringes upon the interests of creditors

blocks.content: In (2024) Yue 19 Min Zhong No. 853 Civil Judgment, the court pointed out that when the shareholder transferred the equity within the term of capital contribution, the company was in normal business operation. Although it owed debts, there was no evidence to prove that the company was unable to pay off its due debts at the time of equity transfer, and the transferee was not obviously lacking the ability to pay capital contribution. Such equity transfer was a normal commercial act. Therefore, the court did not determine that the shareholder has the malice to evade the obligation of capital contribution when transferring the equity within the term of capital contribution, and thus did not order it to bear responsibility.

However, in the case of (2020) Jing 03 Min Zhong No. 3634, although the shareholder has not reached the time limit for capital contribution when transferring his equity, the shareholder knows that the company cannot pay off the due debts at the time of transfer, and the transferee obviously lacks the ability to pay capital contribution. Such equity transfer increases the risk of the paid-in registered capital of the company and affects the realization of the creditor's rights due to the company. Obviously, it is a malicious evasion of the obligation of capital contribution by means of equity transfer, and the transferor shall be liable for capital contribution according to law.

blocks.text: (2) Disputes over capital contribution liability arising from the transfer of equity by shareholders within the term of capital contribution after July 1, 2024
1. Statutory circumstances under which liability must be assumed

blocks.content: When a shareholder transfers the equity within the term of capital contribution, if the transferee fails to pay the capital contribution in full and on time, the original shareholder shall bear supplementary liability for the unpaid part, excluding interest. In the case of (2024) Nei 04 Min Zhong No. 5215, the court of second instance held that the debt between the creditor and the company occurred during the period when the original shareholder was a shareholder of the company, and the original shareholder transferred his equity without fully fulfilling his obligation to make capital contributions. If the company was unable to pay off its debts and enforce its final capital, the court applied for adding the original shareholder as the person subjected to execution in accordance with the law.

blocks.text: If the original shareholder fails to pay the capital contribution in accordance with the articles of association or the non-monetary assets are significantly insufficient, even if the equity has been transferred, he shall still be jointly and severally liable with the transferee within the scope of insufficient capital contribution.
2. Possible Paths for Disclaimer

If the original shareholder can prove that the capital contribution has been paid in full or there is no defect, or that the creditor's rights are formed after the transfer of equity, the original shareholder may claim exemption based on the protection of trust interests. In addition, if the limitation of action has expired when the creditor claims the debt, the original shareholder may be exempted from liability by claiming the defense of limitation.
Suggestions on Optimizing the Protection Route of Creditors' Interests
Litigation stage
Thru Thru Recourse
Bring a lawsuit against the original shareholder and the transferee at the same time (especially in the case of false capital contribution)
Make good use of Article 35 of the Enterprise Bankruptcy Law to accelerate the maturity
Key Points for Evidence Collection
Access to industrial and commercial internal files to verify investment commitments
Obtain the certificate of payment of the equity transfer price
Collect the chain of evidence for the withdrawal of capital by the original shareholders
(2) Execution stage
Provisions on Several Issues Concerning the Alteration and Addition of Parties in Civil Execution

Article 19 Where the property of a company as the person subjected to execution is insufficient to pay off the debts specified in the effective legal documents, and its shareholder fails to perform its obligation of capital contribution in accordance with the law and transfers its equity, the applicant for execution may apply to the people's court for changing or adding the original shareholder or the initiator who shall bear joint and several liability for the capital contribution in accordance with the provisions of the Company Law as the person subjected to execution, and the people's court shall support it if it is liable within the scope of capital contribution not made in accordance with the law.
The original shareholder shall be changed or added as the person subjected to execution, so as to make him liable within the scope of capital contribution not made according to law.

references: Company Law of the People's Republic of China
Enterprise Bankruptcy Law of the People's Republic of China
Provisions of the Supreme People's Court on Several Issues Concerning the Application of the Company Law of the People's Republic of China (III) (Revised in 2020)
Provisions on Several Issues Concerning the Alteration and Addition of Parties in Civil Execution
Several Provisions of the Supreme People's Court on the Time Effect of the Application of the Company Law of the People's Republic of China
en.subtitle: — Article 88 of the Company Law
en.conclusion: The change of the liability rules for the transfer of unexpired equity under the new Company Law has put forward new requirements for all parties. Whether the original shareholders, transferees or creditors, they should accurately grasp the changes in rules, rationally plan commercial acts and prevent legal risks. If you encounter relevant problems in practice, you are welcome to contact us, and Beijing Qingsong Law Firm will provide you with professional legal services.

zh.title: 破解股东利用知识产权虚假出资的“金蝉脱壳”术：法律路径与实务指南
zh.lead: 小股东以一款游戏的知识产权作价300万出资。但债权人发现，该知识产权实际价值远低于认缴金额，导致公司无财产可供执行...

blocks.text: ——公司无财产可供执行？债权人如何绝地反击
问题背景：股东“空手套白狼”的典型套路
注册资本3900万的公司，小股东以一款游戏的知识产权作价300万出资。但债权人发现，该知识产权实际价值远低于认缴金额，导致公司无财产可供执行，股东却试图通过“评估报告”逃避责任。债权人虽已胜诉并追加股东为被执行人，但股东提出执行异议之诉，案件陷入僵局。
核心矛盾点：如何证明知识产权出资不实？如何突破原有评估报告的“合法性”？
法律武器库：击穿虚假出资的三大核心依据
非货币出资的法定要求——权属转移+评估作价
根据《公司法》第48、49条及《公司法司法解释三》第9、10条，股东可以用实物、知识产权、土地使用权、股权、债权等可以用货币估价并可以依法转让的非货币财产作价出资，以知识产权出资需完成两项义务：
✅权属变更：将知识产权过户至公司名下；
✅评估作价：由合法评估机构出具报告，不得虚高作价。
法律后果：未完成权属转移或评估价显著偏低的，视为未履行出资义务，股东需补足差额。
股东出资不实的连带责任
《公司法》第二十三条第一款　公司股东滥用公司法人独立地位和股东有限责任，逃避债务，严重损害公司债权人利益的，应当对公司债务承担连带责任。
债权人可依据《公司法》第50条或者《公司法司法解释三》第13条，要求股东在未出资本息范围内对公司债务承担补充责任。
此外若股东滥用权利转移资产、逃避债务，严重损害公司债权人利益的，受到损害的债权人可根据《公司法》第23条主张股东与公司人格混同，要求股东承担连带责任。
破解路径：四步击穿股东“障眼法”
Step 1：申请调查令以及财产保全
股东转移资产具有隐蔽性，债权人首先需要在诉讼阶段同步申请财产保全。
其次是通过申请调查令获取该公司的工商内档，重点核查工商档案中的知识产权评估报告、知识产权登记信息、关联交易记录、权属变更记录、验资档案等。在此步骤中我们可以发现该股东用作出资的知识产权价值线索以及其中的漏洞，为下一步申请司法审计做好准备。

blocks.content: 如（2020）鲁15民再23号案中，股东未完成知识产权权属变更，法院直接认定未履行出资义务，承担补充责任。
在(2024)京03民终11534号案中，法官指出在先做出的股东会决议和章程修正案中显示的知识产权出资数额与在后作出的评估报告中知识产权的价值完全吻合，明显不合常理；其次是股东在债务危机时将货币出资变更为专利，法院以该行为降低了财产的流动性，客观上损害了债权人的利益为由，判令该行为不产生出资的法律效力。
在(2023)渝0107执异311号案中，法院认为在判断股东是否履行非专利技术出资时，还应当审查该非专利技术与公司经营目的的相关性，以防止股东“花钱买包装”，用虚假非专利技术作价出资，以合法的形式掩盖不真实的出资。这也可以作为判断股东是否是利用知识产权虚假出资的一个重要依据。

blocks.text: Step 2：申请司法审计，撕开虚假评估面纱

blocks.title: 《最高人民法院关于民事执行中财产调查若干问题的规定》（2020修正）
blocks.content: 第十七条 作为被执行人的法人或非法人组织不履行生效法律文书确定的义务，申请执行人认为其有拒绝报告、虚假报告财产情况，隐匿、转移财产等逃避债务情形或者其股东、出资人有出资不实、抽逃出资等情形的，可以书面申请人民法院委托审计机构对该被执行人进行审计。人民法院应当自收到书面申请之日起十日内决定是否准许。

blocks.text: 实务难点与破解技巧
难点1：如何说服法官启动重新评估？
通过申请调查令，获取知识产权评估报告、权属变更记录、验资档案等线索找出该出资可能存在的瑕疵，如：
债权人可通过比较市场同类知识产权交易价格、实际收益数据、取得知识产权的成本价格等证明知识产权实际价值与认缴金额差异巨大；
若知识产权未实际交付使用（如游戏未上线运营）或者该知识产权与该公司经营目的无任何相关性，可据此主张出资未完成；
提供原评估机构的“黑历史”（如行政处罚记录），直接主张原报告不可信，要求重新评估；
若该知识产权的评估报告存在未披露关键限制条件、评估参数不合理等情形，可据此主张评估程序违法；
难点2：股东主张“市场波动导致贬值”如何应对？
若股东依据《公司法司法解释三》第15条主张因市场变化导致的贬值，股东无需补足，并且表示贬值非主观恶意造成。
债权人可通过举证知识产权自始不具备高价值（如技术过时、无实际应用场景），否定“市场波动”抗辩。
Step3：申请宣告股东变更出资方式行为无效，追回转移资产
在公司债务产生后，公司债权人亦已经通过诉讼的方式要求股东出资加速到期的情况下，股东通过股东会决议变更股东出资方式逃避货币出资义务，属于滥用股东权利损害公司债权人利益的行为，为公司法所禁止，但对于该行为的法律效力，公司法没有明确规定。《最高人民法院关于适用〈中华人民共和国民法典〉总则编若干问题的解释》第三条第三款规定，构成滥用民事权利的，人民法院应当认定该滥用行为不发生相应的法律效力。

blocks.content: 在（2024）京民终3号中，股东在公司债务对外不能清偿的情况下，将货币出资变更为非货币的知识产权出资，由于《公司法》规定出资方式是公示内容，债权人是依据公示信息决定是否与公司发生交易，债权人对于出资方式具有信赖利益。非货币出资，尤其是知识产权出资，相比货币出资，其清偿能力明显受限，因此，在债务发生后股东变更出资，对债权人没有约束力，债权人仍然有权要求股东继续货币出资。

zh.conclusion: 股东利用知识产权虚假出资的“合法外衣”终将被法律击穿！债权人需善用司法审计、追加被执行人、撤销权诉讼等工具，结合刑事追责形成高压态势。唯有专业布局+快速行动，方能破解执行僵局，实现债权回款。

en.title: Cracking the "Golden Cicada Deblocking" Technique of Shareholders Using Intellectual Property Rights to Make False Capital Contributions: Legal Paths and Practical Guidelines
en.lead: The minority shareholder makes a capital contribution of 3 million yuan with the intellectual property right of a game. However, the creditor finds that the actual value of the intellectual property right is far lower than the subscribed amount, resulting in no property for the company to enforce.

blocks.text: — The company has no property to enforce? How creditors fight back
Background of the problem: the typical pattern of shareholders' "empty handed white wolf"

For a company with a registered capital of 39 million yuan, the minority shareholder shall make a capital contribution of 3 million yuan with the intellectual property right of a game. However, the creditor finds that the actual value of the intellectual property right is far lower than the subscribed amount, resulting in no property available for execution by the company, and the shareholder tries to evade liability through the "evaluation report". Although the creditor has won the lawsuit and added the shareholder as the person subjected to execution, the shareholder has filed a lawsuit against the execution, and the case has reached a deadlock.
Core contradiction: How to prove that the intellectual property investment is untrue? How to break through the "legality" of the original evaluation report?
Legal Weapons: Three Core Bases for Cracking Down on False Capital Contributions
Statutory requirements for non-monetary capital contribution — transfer of ownership + valuation

In accordance with Articles 48 and 49 of the Company Law and Articles 9 and 10 of Judicial Interpretation III of the Company Law, shareholders may make capital contributions in kind, intellectual property rights, land use rights, equity, creditor's rights and other non-monetary property that can be valued in currency and transferred according to law, and two obligations shall be fulfilled for intellectual property rights:
✅ Change of ownership: transfer the intellectual property right to the company;
✅ Appraisal and pricing: a report shall be issued by a legal appraisal institution, and the price shall not be falsely overvalued.

Legal consequences: If the transfer of ownership has not been completed or the appraisal price is significantly low, it shall be deemed that it has not fulfilled its obligation of capital contribution, and the shareholders shall make up the difference.
Joint and several liability for false capital contribution of shareholders

Paragraph 1 of Article 23 of the Company Law. Where a shareholder of a company abuses the independent status of a company as a juridical person and the limited liability of a shareholder to evade debts and seriously damages the interests of the creditors of the company, he shall bear joint and several liabilities for the debts of the company.

The creditor may, in accordance with Article 50 of the Company Law or Article 13 of the Judicial Interpretation III of the Company Law, require the shareholder to assume supplementary liability for the debts of the company within the scope of the principal and interest not contributed.

In addition, if a shareholder abuses his right to transfer assets or evade debts, which seriously damages the interests of the creditors of the company, the damaged creditor may, in accordance with Article 23 of the Company Law, claim that the shareholder is confused with the company's personality and require the shareholder to bear joint and several liability.
Cracking the Way: Four Steps to Break through the "Obstruction" of Shareholders
Step 1: Application for Investigation Order and Property Preservation
The transfer of assets by shareholders is concealed, and creditors first need to apply for property preservation simultaneously at the litigation stage.

Next, obtain the industrial and commercial internal files of the company by applying for an investigation order, focusing on the verification of intellectual property evaluation reports, intellectual property registration information, related party transaction records, ownership change records and capital verification files in the industrial and commercial archives. In this step, we can find the clues of the value of the intellectual property used by the shareholder as capital contribution and the loopholes therein, so as to prepare for the next step of applying for judicial audit.

blocks.content: If the shareholder fails to complete the change of ownership of intellectual property rights in the (2020) Case No. 23 of the 15th People's Court of Shandong Province, the court directly determines that it has not fulfilled its obligation of capital contribution and assumes supplementary liability.

In Case No. 11534 [2024] of Jing 03 Civil Division, the judge pointed out that the amount of intellectual property investment shown in the resolution of the shareholders' meeting and the amendment to the articles of association made earlier was completely consistent with the value of intellectual property in the evaluation report made later, which was obviously unreasonable; Secondly, when the shareholder changes the monetary capital contribution into a patent during the debt crisis, the court ruled that the act would not have the legal effect of capital contribution on the grounds that the act reduced the liquidity of property and objectively damaged the interests of creditors.

In the case of (2023) Yu 0107 Zhiyi No. 311, the court held that when judging whether a shareholder has fulfilled the capital contribution of non-patented technology, it should also examine the relevance of the non-patented technology to the business purpose of the company to prevent shareholders from "spending money to buy packages", making capital contributions at a price of false non-patented technology, and covering up untrue capital contributions in legal form. This can also be used as an important basis for judging whether shareholders make false capital contributions by using intellectual property rights.

blocks.text: Step 2: Apply for Judicial Audit and Peel the Veil of False Assessment

blocks.title: Provisions of the Supreme People's Court on Several Issues Concerning Property Investigation in Civil Execution (2020 Amendment)
blocks.content: Article 17 If a juridical person or unincorporated organization as the person subjected to execution fails to perform the obligations specified in the effective legal documents, and the applicant for execution considers that it has evaded its debts by refusing to report, falsely reporting its property, concealing or transferring its property, or that its shareholders or investors have made false capital contributions or withdrawn their capital contributions, it may apply in writing to the people's court for entrusting an audit institution to audit the person subjected to execution. The people's court shall decide whether to grant or not within ten days from the date of receipt of the written application.

blocks.text: Practical Difficulties and Cracking Skills
Difficulty 1: How can the judge be persuaded to initiate a reassessment?

Obtain clues such as intellectual property appraisal reports, ownership change records and capital verification files by applying for investigation orders to find out possible defects in the capital contribution, such as:

Creditors may prove that the actual value of intellectual property rights differs greatly from the amount subscribed by comparing the transaction price, actual income data and cost price of obtaining intellectual property rights of the same kind in the market;

If the intellectual property is not actually delivered for use (such as the game is not online for operation) or the intellectual property has no relevance to the business purpose of the company, it may be claimed that the capital contribution has not been completed accordingly;
Provide the "black history" of the original assessment institution (such as administrative penalty records), directly claim that the original report is not trustworthy, and request a new assessment;

If the evaluation report of the intellectual property right does not disclose the key restrictions and the evaluation parameters are unreasonable, it may be claimed that the evaluation procedure is illegal;
Difficulty 2: How to deal with the shareholder's claim that "market fluctuations lead to depreciation"?

If the shareholder claims the depreciation caused by market changes in accordance with Article 15 of Judicial Interpretation III of the Company Law, the shareholder does not need to make up for it, and indicates that the depreciation is not caused by subjective malice.

Creditors may deny the "market fluctuation" defense by proving that intellectual property rights do not have high value from the beginning (such as outdated technology and no practical application scenario).
Step 3: The application for declaring the shareholder's change in the mode of capital contribution invalid and recovering the transferred assets

After the company's debts arise, and the company's creditors have already requested the shareholders to accelerate the expiration of their capital contributions through litigation, if the shareholders change the mode of capital contribution through a shareholders' resolution to evade the obligation of monetary capital contribution, it is an act of abusing shareholders' rights to harm the interests of the company's creditors, which is prohibited by the Company Law. However, the Company Law does not explicitly stipulate the legal effect of such acts. Paragraph 3 of Article 3 of the Interpretation of the Supreme People's Court on Several Issues Concerning the Application of the General Principles of the Civil Code of the People's Republic of China stipulates that if an abuse of civil rights is constituted, the people's court shall determine that the abuse does not have corresponding legal effect.

blocks.content: In (2024) Jing Min Zhong No. 3, when the company's debts cannot be paid off to the outside world, the shareholders change the monetary capital contribution into non-monetary intellectual property capital contribution. As the Company Law stipulates that the mode of capital contribution is publicity, the creditor decides whether to trade with the company according to the publicity information, and the creditor has a trust interest in the mode of capital contribution. Non-monetary capital contribution, especially intellectual property capital contribution, is obviously limited in its solvency compared with monetary capital contribution. Therefore, if the shareholder changes the capital contribution after the debt occurs, it shall not be binding on the creditor, and the creditor shall still have the right to request the shareholder to continue to make monetary capital contribution.

en.conclusion: The "legal cloak" of shareholders who make false capital contributions by using intellectual property rights will eventually be broken down by law! Creditors should make good use of such tools as judicial audit, additional executors and revocation litigation to form a high-pressure situation in combination with criminal accountability. Only professional layout + rapid action can solve the implementation deadlock and realize the collection of creditor's rights.

zh.title: 房产登记隐匿执行实务解析：穿透代持面纱的四大场景与法律路径
zh.lead: 房产登记在他人名下绝非“避债保险箱”！债权人只需抓住出资来源、实际控制、债务时间线三大核心，配合法律工具的灵活运用，即可通过法律程序否定登记外观主义效力，让隐匿财产无所遁形...

blocks.text: 引言
在民事执行程序中，有的被执行人为逃避债务，将实际由自己出资购买房产登记在配偶、子女甚至其他第三人名下，导致法院执行时“无财产可供执行”——这一现象屡见不鲜。但房产登记≠真实权属！本文结合《民法典》及司法解释，以司法判例为支撑，拆解“借名隐匿财产”的四大场景与破解之道，为债权人提供穿透式执行方案。
四大隐匿场景与破解路径
场景1：房产登记在配偶名下——代位析产分割共有物＆撤销不当处分
对于被执行人将其房产或者属于夫妻共同财产的房产通过离婚协议等方式分割，登记在配偶名下的财产是否可以被直接执行呢？
若该财产虽被登记在配偶名下，但有直接证据证明是夫妻共同财产，依据《最高人民法院关于人民法院民事执行中查封、扣押、冻结财产的规定》第十二条，法院可直接查封共有房产，并及时通知共有人，共有人可以协议分割财产，也可以提起共有人析产之诉，若共有人怠于分割财产导致债权人权益受损的，债权人可直接提起债权人代为析产之诉。
若配偶主张个人财产，债权人可代位提起析产诉讼，确认债务人所占财产份额。若被执行人通过离婚协议无偿转让财产的，债权人可提起撤销权之诉

blocks.content: 在(2024)云01民终959号案中，法院认为，在债务产生后，债务人通过案涉《离婚协议书》无偿处分案涉房屋的行为已经影响债权人的债权实现，该约定严重削弱了债务人的清偿能力,故于法院支持了撤销案涉《离婚协议书》第五条“财产分割”第2款的诉讼请求。

blocks.text: 场景2：房产登记在第三人名下——撤销权之诉
如果被执行人签约负债之时，具有较高的清偿能力，债权人基于被执行人较佳的商誉情况，才决定与被执行人进行交易，但是，债权债务产生之后，被执行人将财产赠与、或者以不合理低价转让给他人，会导致其清偿能力下降，该无偿赠与行为损害了债权人利益，因此，债权人可以起诉撤销该赠与或转让行为。

blocks.content: 在(2021)豫05民终888号案中在，债务人在债务发生后以明显不合理的低价将诉争房屋转让给第三人，损害了债权人利益的实现，故法院支持了债权人要求撤销被告与第三人签订的《房屋买卖协议》，并确认涉案房产为债务人所有的主张。

blocks.text: 但是相反，如果被执行人签约之前，早已经将财产赠与他人，由于在签订负债合同之时，债权人考察了对方（即被执行人）的商誉情况后，仍然决定发生商务往来，这属于债权人未尽审慎注意义务，应自行承担交易风险，不得撤销被执行人的赠与行为。
场景3：房产登记在未成年子女名下——确认之诉认定为家庭共同财产
首先，一般来说，未成年子女无收入，购房款一般来源于父母。
云阳县人民法院认为根据《民法典》第五百九十五条规定，“买卖合同是出卖人转移标的物的所有权于买受人，买受人支付价款的合同”，根据购房合同及有关付款收据等证据判断，实际支付了购买标的物价款的，应是买卖合同的实际买受人。

如果购房款由子女支付，则审查该子女有无实际购买能力。未成年子女没有独立的经济来源，基本没有购买能力，则应该重点讨论该未成年子女名下财产的性质。未成年子女作为家庭的一员，其名下财产无论来源于夫妻任何一方，一般都属于家庭共有的财产。但是也不排除部分未成年子女的确享有属于个人的财产，从保护债权人的合法权益，维护交易安全的角度，这一类财产无疑应当认定属于未成年子女所有，法院在办理执行案件时不应将该类财产列为执行的范围，亦不得对其采取冻结、查封等强制措施。
其次，虽然房屋系登记在未成年子女名下，但房屋大多都由家庭成员长期共同居住，由父母管理房产，应当视为家庭共同财产。

blocks.content: 在（2022）湘0725民初3288号案例中，法院将房屋现有使用现状作为判断房屋实际权属的依据之一。

blocks.text: 最后房产登记时间与债务形成时间的前后是审查债务人是否恶意的一个重要因素，也是债权人行使撤销权的一个重要依据。
若房产登记在债务之后，根据《民法典》第一百五十四条的规定：“行为人与相对人恶意串通，损害他人合法权益的民事法律行为无效。”可直接推定恶意转移；申请执行人可要通过债权人撤销之诉撤销赠与使前述房屋回归被执行人所有，然后再执行前述房屋。

blocks.content: 若房产登记在债务发生之前，（2020）最高法民申6800号民事裁定认为，被执行人出资购买登记在未成年子女名下的房屋是家庭共有财产，被执行人所有的份额，可以被执行。湖南省桃源县人民法院在在（2022）湘0725民初3288号案中，认为在无充分证据证明债务人夫妻在购房时真实意思是将房屋赠予其未成年女儿；另其未成年女儿亦未提供证据证明其对案涉房屋进行了投入或贡献的情况下，法院结合案涉房屋实际出资、现有使用现状、社会现状等实际情况，同时从平衡债权人、债务人双方利益的角度考量，认定该房屋系债务人夫妻的的夫妻共同财产。

blocks.text: 场景4：房产登记在成年子女名下——确认之诉认定为家庭共同财产
成年子女需自证购房能力，否则仍可能被认定代持！购房款是否来源于债务人是判断房屋实际权属的一个重要依据，对于成年子女而言应该重点审查其工资收入水平和涉案财产是否呈正相关关系，如大额转账、债务形成前财产状况等。

(2020)最高法民申5648号案中，最高人民法院认为，被执行人子女购房时不具相应能力支付购房款，案涉房屋的款项系被执行人支付。但被执行人子女购买案涉房屋的时间，债权人与被执行人之间的债权债务并未发生，不能证明被执行人为子女购房系恶意逃避债务行为。案涉房屋为债务人夫妇出资购买，购买房屋时其成年女儿没有经济能力购买近百万元价值的房屋，案涉房屋的实际出资人不是权利登记人，因此，法院将案涉房屋认定为是债务人夫妇与其成年女儿的家庭共有财产，被执行人所有的份额，可以被执行。
律师办案工具箱：执行程序全流程指引
财产线索挖掘与证据链构建
申请调查令：调取债务人及关联人银行流水、购房合同、房产登记信息、物业记录；找出购房款出资凭证与实际占有使用证明。
穿透资金流向：根据债务人财产报告，重点核查债务形成前后的异常转账。
诉讼策略选择
撤销权之诉：针对无偿转让、恶意转移

blocks.content: 在（2023）渝0231民初2731号案中，债务人试图通过签署《离婚协议书》无偿处分案涉房屋，严重损害了债权人的合法权益，法院支持了债权人要求撤销债务人通过离婚协议的约定的诉讼请求。

blocks.text: 代位析产诉讼：针对夫妻、家庭共有财产，且债务人怠于分割财产

blocks.content: 在(2024)陕0111民初5035号案、(2025)云01民终518号案中，相关债权处于法院的强制执行中，并且债务人除与案外人共有财产外，无其他可供执行的财产，法院已查封、扣押、冻结该共有财产并通知共有人，被执行人、其他共有人既不协议分割共有财产亦不提起析产诉讼，对法院的强制执行和申请执行人的债权实现构成了妨碍，法院支持了债权人要求对共有财产进行析产的诉讼请求，维护债权人的合法权益。

blocks.text: 活用“拒执罪”施压：对恶意转移情节严重者，追究刑责

blocks.content: （2025）新40刑终9号案中，法院认为，被告人陈某甲为了逃避责任，在收到法院追加其为被执行人通知后，明知其房产有银行贷款未还清，财产不足以向申请执行人杨某乙清偿债务，仍出售房产，并要求购买人将出售款以现金方式支付给其儿子，转移、隐匿财产逃避法院执行的主观故意明显，致使人民法院生效的裁定无法执行，属于情节严重，已构成拒不执行判决、裁定罪，应依法惩处。

blocks.text: 执行衔接措施
根据《最高人民法院关于人民法院民事执行中查封、扣押、冻结财产的规定》第十三条第一、二款的规定同步申请查封第三人名下房产，防止变卖；以生效判决直接申请执行，避免程序空转。

zh.conclusion:
房产登记在他人名下绝非“避债保险箱”！债权人只需抓住出资来源、实际控制、债务时间线三大核心，配合法律工具的灵活运用，即可通过法律程序否定登记外观主义效力，让隐匿财产无所遁形！
（文中案例当事人均为化名，如需个案咨询请联系专业执行律师）
——专注执行领域10年，我们让判决落地有声——
参考资料：
《中华人民共和国民法典》
第一百五十四条 行为人与相对人恶意串通，损害他人合法权益的民事法律行为无效。
第三百零三条　共有人约定不得分割共有的不动产或者动产，以维持共有关系的，应当按照约定，但是共有人有重大理由需要分割的，可以请求分割；没有约定或者约定不明确的，按份共有人可以随时请求分割，共同共有人在共有的基础丧失或者有重大理由需要分割时可以请求分割。因分割造成其他共有人损害的，应当给予赔偿。
第五百三十八条 债务人以放弃其债权、放弃债权担保、无偿转让财产等方式无偿处分财产权益，或者恶意延长其到期债权的履行期限，影响债权人的债权实现的，债权人可以请求人民法院撤销债务人的行为。
第五百四十一条 撤销权自债权人知道或者应当知道撤销事由之日起一年内行使。自债务人的行为发生之日起五年内没有行使撤销权的，该撤销权消灭。
《中华人民共和国刑法》
第三百一十三条　【拒不执行判决、裁定罪】对人民法院的判决、裁定有能力执行而拒不执行，情节严重的，处三年以下有期徒刑、拘役或者罚金；情节特别严重的，处三年以上七年以下有期徒刑，并处罚金。
《最高人民法院关于人民法院民事执行中查封、扣押、冻结财产的规定》
第十二条 对被执行人与其他人共有的财产，人民法院可以查封、扣押、冻结，并及时通知共有人。
共有人协议分割共有财产，并经债权人认可的，人民法院可以认定有效。查封、扣押、冻结的效力及于协议分割后被执行人享有份额内的财产；对其他共有人享有份额内的财产的查封、扣押、冻结，人民法院应当裁定予以解除。
共有人提起析产诉讼或者申请执行人代位提起析产诉讼的，人民法院应当准许。诉讼期间中止对该财产的执行。
第十三条第一、二款 对第三人为被执行人的利益占有的被执行人的财产，人民法院可以查封、扣押、冻结；该财产被指定给第三人继续保管的，第三人不得将其交付给被执行人。
对第三人为自己的利益依法占有的被执行人的财产，人民法院可以查封、扣押、冻结，第三人可以继续占有和使用该财产，但不得将其交付给被执行人。

en.title: Interpretation of the Implementation Practice of Real Estate Registration and Concealment: Four Scenes and Legal Paths to Pierce the Veil
en.lead: Real estate registered in the name of others is definitely not a" debt avoidance safe "!Creditors only need to grasp the three cores of the source of capital contribution, actual control and debt time line, and cooperate with the flexible use of legal tools, they can negate the effect of registration facade through legal procedures, so that hidden property has no hiding place..

blocks.text: Introduction

In the civil execution procedure, some persons subjected to execution, in order to evade their debts, register the purchase of real estate actually funded by themselves in the name of their spouses, children or even other third parties, resulting in" no property available for execution "when the court executes it - a common phenomenon.However, the real estate registration is not equal to the real ownership!This article, based on the Civil Code and judicial interpretations and supported by judicial precedents, dismantles the four scenarios of "concealing property by name" and the ways to solve them, so as to provide creditors with a penetrating execution plan.
Four Hidden Scenes and Cracking Paths
Scenario 1: Real Estate Registered in Spouse's Name – Subdivision of Common Property by Subrogation&Cancellation of Improper Punishment

Can the property registered in the name of the spouse be directly executed if the person subjected to execution divides his or her real estate or the real estate belonging to the common property of the husband and wife by means of divorce agreement?

If the property is registered in the name of the spouse, but there is direct evidence to prove that it is the joint property of the husband and wife, according to Article 12 of the Provisions of the Supreme People's Court on Sealing up, Seizure and Freezing Property in Civil Execution by the People's Court, the court may directly seal up the jointly owned property and notify the co-owners in a timely manner. The co-owners may agree to divide the property or bring a lawsuit against the co-owners for property division. If the co-owners are lazy in dividing the property and cause damage to the rights and interests of the creditors, the creditors may directly bring a lawsuit against the creditors for property division on their behalf.

If the spouse claims personal property, the creditor may bring a lawsuit of property division by subrogation to confirm the debtor's share of property.If the person subjected to execution transfers his property free of charge through a divorce agreement, the creditor may bring an action for cancellation right

blocks.content: In case No. 959 [2024] of Yun01 Civil Division, the court held that after the debt was incurred, the debtor's gratuitous disposal of the house involved in the case through the divorce agreement had affected the realization of the creditor's rights, and the agreement seriously weakened the debtor's ability to pay off, so the court supported the claim to revoke the case involving the second paragraph of Article 5" Property Division "of the Divorce Agreement.

blocks.text: Scene 2: Real Estate Registered in the Name of a Third Person – Revocation Action

If the person subjected to execution has a high solvency at the time of signing the contract for debts, the creditor decides to trade with the person subjected to execution only on the basis of the better goodwill of the person subjected to execution. However, after the creditor's rights and debts are incurred, if the person subjected to execution donates property or transfers it to others at an unreasonable low price, his solvency will be reduced, and the gratuitous donation will damage the interests of the creditor. Therefore, the creditor may bring a lawsuit to revoke the donation or transfer.

blocks.content: In the case of (2021) Yu 05 Min Zhong No.888, the debtor transferred the house in dispute to a third party at an obviously unreasonable low price after the debt occurred, thus harming the realization of the interests of the creditor, so the court supported the creditor's claim to revoke the House Sale Agreement signed by the defendant and the third party, and confirmed that the real estate involved in the case was owned by the debtor.

blocks.text: However, conversely, if the person subjected to execution had already donated property to others before signing the contract, and at the time of entering into the debt contract, the creditor had examined the other party's (i.e., the person subjected to execution's) goodwill and still decided to conduct business, this constitutes the creditor's failure to exercise due diligence, and the creditor should bear the transaction risk itself and may not revoke the gift of the person subjected to execution.
Scene 3: Real estate registered in the name of minor children - confirmation action is recognized as family common property
First of all, generally speaking, minor children have no income and the purchase money generally comes from their parents.

The People's Court of Yunyang County considers that according to Article 595 of the Civil Code," a sales contract is a contract whereby the seller transfers the ownership of the subject matter to the buyer and the buyer pays the price ". According to the purchase contract and relevant payment receipts and other evidence, if the price of the purchase subject matter is actually paid, it shall be the actual buyer of the sales contract.

If the purchase price is paid by the child, examine whether the child has the actual purchasing power.If a minor child has no independent source of income and basically has no purchasing power, the nature of the property under the minor child's name should be emphasized.As a member of the family, the property under the name of a minor child, regardless of whether it comes from either party of the husband and wife, generally belongs to the common property of the family.However, it does not rule out that some minor children do enjoy personal property. From the perspective of protecting the legitimate rights and interests of creditors and safeguarding the safety of transactions, such property should undoubtedly be recognized as owned by minor children, and the court shall not list such property as the scope of execution when handling enforcement cases, nor shall it take compulsory measures such as freezing and sealing up.

Secondly, although the house is registered under the name of the minor child, most of the houses are shared by family members for a long time, and the real estate managed by the parents shall be regarded as the common property of the family.

blocks.content: In the case No. 3288 [2022] of the early Republic of China of Xiang 0725, the court took the existing use status of the house as one of the bases for judging the actual ownership of the house.

blocks.text: The time of final real estate registration and the time before and after the formation of the debt is an important factor in examining whether the debtor is malicious, and also an important basis for the creditor to exercise the right of revocation.

If the real estate is registered after the debt, according to the provisions of Article 154 of the Civil Code:" A civil juristic act of malicious collusion between the actor and the counterpart that damages the legitimate rights and interests of others is invalid. "Malicious transfer may be directly presumed;The applicant for execution may revoke the gift through the creditor's action for revocation to make the above-mentioned house return to the owner of the executed person, and then execute the above-mentioned house.

blocks.content: If the real estate is registered before the debt occurs, the Civil Ruling No. (2020) of the Supreme People's Court holds that the house registered in the name of the minor child purchased by the person subjected to execution is the common property of the family, and the shares owned by the person subjected to execution may be executed.The People's Court of Taoyuan County, Hunan Province, in Case No. 3288 [2022] of the early Republic of China, held that there was no sufficient evidence to prove that the debtor's husband and wife really meant to donate the house to their minor daughter when purchasing the house;In addition, if his minor daughter has not provided evidence to prove that he has invested or contributed to the house involved in the case, the court shall determine that the house is the joint property of the debtor's husband and wife from the perspective of balancing the interests of both creditors and debtors in light of the actual investment, existing use status and social status of the house involved in the case.

blocks.text: Scene 4: Real estate is registered in the name of an adult child - the action for confirmation is recognized as the common property of the family

Adult children need to prove their ability to purchase houses by themselves, otherwise they may still be recognized as holding on their behalf!Whether the purchase money comes from the debtor is an important basis for judging the actual ownership of the house. For adult children, we should focus on examining whether their wage income level is positively related to the property involved, such as large transfer and property status before the formation of debts.

(2020) In the Supreme People's Court Case No. 5648, the Supreme People's Court held that the children of the person subjected to execution did not have the corresponding ability to pay the purchase price when purchasing the house, and the money involved in the case was paid by the person subjected to execution.However, when the children of the person subjected to execution purchased the house involved in the case, the creditor's rights and debts between the creditor and the person subjected to execution did not occur, and it cannot be proved that the purchase of a house for the children of the person subjected to execution was a malicious evasion of debts.The house involved in the case is purchased by the debtor's husband and wife, and his adult daughter has no financial ability to purchase a house with a value of nearly one million yuan when purchasing the house. The actual investor of the house involved in the case is not the right registrant. Therefore, the court determines that the house involved in the case is the common property of the debtor's husband and wife and their adult daughter's family, and the shares owned by the person subjected to execution may be executed.
Lawyers' Case Handling Tool Box: Guidelines for the Whole Process of Enforcement Procedures
Property clues mining and evidence chain construction

Application for investigation order: obtain the bank flow, house purchase contract, real estate registration information and property records of the debtor and related parties;Find out the certificate of capital contribution for the purchase price and the certificate of actual possession and use.
Through the flow of funds: According to the debtor's property report, focus on the verification of abnormal transfers before and after the formation of debts.
Litigation Strategy Selection
Revocation right action: against gratuitous transfer or malicious transfer

blocks.content: In Case No. 2731 [2023] of the Early Republic of China, the debtor attempted to dispose of the house involved in the case free of charge by signing the Divorce Agreement, which seriously damaged the legitimate rights and interests of the creditor, and the court supported the creditor's claim to revoke the debtor's agreement through the divorce agreement.

blocks.text: Subrogation of property division litigation: against the common property of husband, wife and family, and the debtor is negligent in dividing the property

blocks.content: In the (2024) Shaan 0111 Early Republic of China Case No. 5035 and (2025) Yun 01 Civil End Case No. 518, the relevant creditor's rights are in the compulsory execution of the court, and the debtor has no other property available for execution except for the property shared with an outsider. The court has sealed up, seized or frozen the common property and notified the co-owners. The person subjected to execution and other co-owners neither agree to divide the common property nor bring a lawsuit for property division, which hinders the compulsory execution of the court and the realization of the creditor's rights of the applicant for execution. The court supports the creditor's claim for property division, and safeguards the legitimate rights and interests of the creditor.

blocks.text: Use the" crime of refusing to execute "flexibly to exert pressure: those whose malicious transfer is serious shall be investigated for criminal responsibility

blocks.content: In the case of (2025) Xin 40 Xing Zhong No. 9, the court held that the defendant Chen Moujia, in order to evade responsibility, after receiving the notice from the court adding him as the person subjected to execution, knowing that his real estate had unpaid bank loans and that his property was insufficient to pay off the debts to the applicant for execution Yang Mouyi, still sold the real estate and required the purchaser to pay the sale proceeds in cash to his son. The subjective intention to transfer and conceal property to evade court execution was obvious, resulting in the effective ruling of the people's court being unenforceable, which constitutes a serious circumstance and has constituted the crime of refusing to execute judgments and rulings, and should be punished according to law.

blocks.text: Implementation of interface measures

In accordance with the provisions of the first and second paragraphs of Article 13 of the Provisions of the Supreme People's Court on Sealing up, Seizure and Freezing Property in Civil Execution by People's Courts, the real estate under the name of a third party shall be applied for sealing up synchronously to prevent sale;To apply for execution directly with an effective judgment to avoid idling of the procedure.

en.conclusion: Real estate registered in the name of others is definitely not a" safe for avoiding debts "!Creditors only need to grasp the three cores of the source of capital contribution, actual control and debt time line, and cooperate with the flexible use of legal tools, they can negate the validity of registration facade through legal procedures, so that hidden property has nothing to hide!\N (All parties to the case in the text are pseudonyms, please contact a professional enforcement lawyer if you need case consultation)  n-We have been focusing on the field of enforcement for 10 years, and we have made the judgment sound -  nReference:  n Civil Code of the People's Republic of China  n Article 154 Civil juristic acts in which the actor and the counterpart collude maliciously and damage the legitimate rights and interests of others are invalid.\N Article 303 Where the co-owners agree not to divide a commonly owned realty or chattel in order to maintain the relationship of co-ownership, the agreement shall be followed, provided that if the co-owners have major reasons to divide the realty or chattel, they may request the division;If there is no agreement or the agreement is not clear, the co-owners by shares may request the division at any time, and the co-owners may request the division when the common basis is lost or there are major reasons for the division.If the division causes damage to other co-owners, compensation shall be made.\N Article 538 Where a debtor disposes of its property rights and interests free of charge by waiving its creditor's rights, waiving the guarantee of its creditor's rights, or transferring its property free of charge, or maliciously extends the time limit for the performance of its due creditor's rights, thus affecting the realization of the creditor's rights, the creditor may request the people's court to revoke the debtor's act.\N Article 541 The obligee's cancellation right shall be exercised within one year from the date on which the obligee knows or should know the cause for cancellation.If the debtor fails to exercise the right of cancellation within five years from the date of occurrence of the debtor's act, the right of cancellation shall be extinguished.\N Criminal Law of the People's Republic of China  n Article 313 [Crime of Refusing to Execute Judgments or Orders] Whoever refuses to execute a judgment or order of a people's court when he has the ability to do so, if the circumstances are serious, shall be sentenced to fixed-term imprisonment of not more than three years, criminal detention or a fine;If the circumstances are especially serious, he shall be sentenced to fixed-term imprisonment of not less than three years but not more than seven years and shall also be fined.\N Provisions of the Supreme People's Court on Sealing up, Seizure and Freezing of Property in Civil Execution by People's Courts  n Article 12 The people's court may seal up, seize or freeze the property jointly owned by the person subjected to execution and other persons, and notify the co-owners in a timely manner.\N If the co-owners divide the common property by agreement and are approved by the creditors, the people's court may determine it to be valid.The effect of sealing up, distraining or freezing shall extend to the property within the share enjoyed by the person subjected to execution after the division of the agreement;The people's court shall rule to terminate the seizure, seizure or freezing of the property within the share enjoyed by other co-owners.\N If a co-owner brings a lawsuit for division of property or an applicant for execution brings a lawsuit for division of property by subrogation, the people's court shall grant permission.Execution of the property shall be suspended during the proceedings.\N Article 13 The people's court may seal up, distrain or freeze the property of the person subjected to execution possessed by a third party for the interests of the person subjected to execution;If the property is assigned to a third party for continued custody, the third party shall not deliver it to the person subjected to execution.\N The people's court may seal up, distrain or freeze the property of the person subjected to execution possessed by a third party according to law for its own interests, and the third party may continue to possess and use the property, but shall not deliver it to the person subjected to execution.\N (2020) Supreme Famin Shen No.6700  n (2020) Supreme Famin Shen No.5648  n zh.title: 案例分享：案外人误转资金至被执行人账户，能否排除执行？
zh.lead: 在民事执行程序中，执行程序中，案外人因操作失误将款项转入被执行人账户的情形并不少见。此类纠纷的争议焦点在于：“误转”款项是否属于被执行人责任财产？案外人能否通过执行异议程序排除执行？本文结合本所律师代理的一个成功回款的执行案件，解析此类案件的法律规则及实务要点。

blocks.text: 案情简介：50万元货款误转被执行人账户，案外人异议被驳回
在王先生与施某买卖合同纠纷一案中，施某未履行生效法律文书确定的支付义务，于是王先生委托本所律师代为提起强制执行申请。
在执行过程中，施某名下某银行的存款账户被法院冻结。案外人詹某、陈某因与施某之子施小某有业务往来，需支付货款，2025年1月，詹某委托其妻陈某向施小某账户转账50万元，于是，陈某向施某账户转账50万元，附言“灵芝粉款”，意图让施某代施小某收款。因施某账户被冻结，款项无法转出，交易中止。詹某、陈某以转账错误为由，向法院提出执行异议，要求原路退回50万元货款。
争议焦点：
误转款项是否仍属施某责任财产？
案外人能否以“误转”为由排除执行？
律师直击要害：从货币权属到证据规则，三大抗辩筑牢执行防线
货币的“占有即所有”规则——涉案款项所有权已发生转移，属于被执行人施某的可供执行的财产
根据《最高人民法院关于人民法院办理执行异议和复议案件若干问题的规定》第二十五条第一款第（三）项，银行存款的权属以账户登记名称为准。货币作为种类物，遵循“占有即所有”原则，一旦转入被执行人账户即视为其责任财产，法院可依法执行。
误转行为的证明责任——案外人詹某和陈某夫妇提供的证据不足以证明此次转款是误转
陈某明知转入账户为施某所有，其提供的银行账户交易记录仅仅能证明存在转账行为，但无法证明系误转，陈荣平在明知收款方为施深根却仍选择转账的情况下，自称此次转账行为系“误转”，并不符合常理，且转账附言“灵芝粉款”未明确代收性质，故案涉50万元归属施某，属于可执行财产。
法院裁判核心：为何“误转”主张无法阻却执行？

法院认为，《最高人民法院关于人民法院办理执行异议和复议案件若干问题的规定》第二十五条第一款第三项的规定，“案外人对人民法院强制执行的银行存款提出执行异议的，人民法院应当按照金融机构登记的账户名称判断执行异议人是否系权利人。”本案中，被执行人施某有未履行完毕的执行案件，法院冻结被执行人施某的存款账户符合法律规定。案外人詹某、陈某所称的汇入被执行人施某的存款账户内的50万元货款，其在转账前已知晓汇入的账户为被执行人名下银行账户，该行为并不属于错汇、误汇，其主张该款归其所有，无法律依据，法院不予支持。据此，法院驳回了案外人詹某、陈某的异议请求。
执行效果：最终，该50万元货款全部用于执行，王先生债权全额实现！本案于2025年2月执行结案。

误转资金的救济路径
执行异议程序
该程序仅适用于对执行标的享有物权或特殊债权的案外人，因本案中资金进入施某账户后已与其他款项混同，不具有特定化，在事实上无法返还“原物”，无权主张“原物返还”。普通债权（如本案不当得利）无法排除执行。
另诉主张不当得利
根据《最高人民法院关于适用执行程序若干问题的解释》第十四条之规定，詹某、陈某以误转的不当得利请求之债权请求为由，无法请求排除以物权为基础的强制执行行为。
案外人可另案起诉被执行人要求返还款项，但需与其他债权人平等受偿，无优先性。
特殊情形突破“占有即所有”的可能性
若误转款项满足以下条件，案外人可能排除执行：
专款专用账户：如农民工工资专户、保证金账户等，资金具有特定用途且未混同；
账户被特定化：款项进入账户后未被使用，且与被执行人其他资金区分；
明确代收合意：案外人与被执行人签订代收协议，且第三人（实际收款方）确认代收事实。
风险防范建议
转账前双重确认：核对账户名称、账号、开户行，避免仅凭“亲属关系”或“历史交易”盲目转账。
备注明确用途：如为代收代付，应在转账附言中注明“代XX收款”，并留存书面协议、聊天记录等证据。
及时采取保全措施：发现误转后立即报警、申请账户冻结，防止款项被划扣。

references: 《中华人民共和国民事诉讼法》
《最高人民法院关于适用<中华人民共和国民事诉讼法>执行程序若干问题的解释》
《最高人民法院关于人民法院办理执行异议和复议案件若干问题的规定》
zh.conclusion:
误转款项能否排除执行，核心在于资金权属认定及案外人权利性质。货币的“占有即所有”规则具有普适性，案外人仅凭“误转”主张排除执行难获支持。企业和个人在交易中应重视账户合规管理，发生纠纷时及时寻求专业法律帮助，通过精准的法律策略维护权益。
关注我们，获取更多执行异议与救济实务指南！
（本文案例及观点供实务参考，具体案件需结合管辖法院规则分析。）

en.title: Case sharing: Can the execution be ruled out if an outsider wrongly transfers funds to the account of the person subjected to execution?
en.lead: In the civil execution procedure, it is not uncommon for an outsider to transfer funds to the account of the person subjected to execution due to operational errors in the execution procedure.The focus of such disputes is: whether the "wrongly transferred" money belongs to the property of the person subjected to execution?Can an outsider rule out enforcement through the enforcement objection procedure?This article analyzes the legal rules and practical essentials of such cases in light of a successful collection enforcement case represented by our lawyers.

blocks.text: Brief introduction to the case: the payment for goods of 500000 yuan was wrongly transferred to the account of the person subjected to execution, and the objection of the outsider was rejected

In the case of a dispute over a sales contract between Mr. Wang and Mr. Shi, Mr. Shi failed to perform the payment obligation specified in the effective legal document, so Mr. Wang entrusted the lawyer of the firm to file an application for enforcement on his behalf.

In the course of execution, the deposit account of a bank under the name of Shi was frozen by the court.The outsiders, Zhan and Chen, need to pay for the goods due to their business dealings with Shi Xiaomou, Shi's son. In January 2025, Zhan entrusted his wife, Chen, to transfer 500000 yuan to Shi Xiaomou's account. Therefore, Chen transferred 500000 yuan to Shi's account with a postscript of "Ganoderm Powder", intending to let Shi collect money on behalf of Shi Xiaomou.Due to the freezing of Shi's account, the funds cannot be transferred out, and the transaction is suspended.Zhan and Chen raised an objection to the execution to the court on the grounds of wrong transfer and requested the original route to refund 500000 yuan.
Focus of dispute:
Does the amount transferred by mistake still belong to the property of Shi?
Can an outsider rule out enforcement on the grounds of" wrongful transfer "?
Lawyers hit the nail on the head: from the ownership of currency to the rules of evidence, the three defenses build a strong line of defense for enforcement
The" possession is ownership "rule of money-the ownership of the money involved has been transferred and belongs to the enforceable property of Shi A of the person subjected to execution

According to Item (3) of Paragraph 1 of Article 25 of the Provisions of the Supreme People's Court on Several Issues Concerning the Handling of Cases of Objection to Execution and Reconsideration by People's Courts, the ownership of bank deposits shall be subject to the registered name of the account.As a kind of thing, currency follows the principle of "possession is ownership", and once transferred to the account of the person subjected to execution, it shall be regarded as its responsible property, which can be executed by the court according to law.
The burden of proof for the act of wrongful transfer - the evidence provided by the outsiders, Mr. and Mrs. Zhan and Mr. Chen, is insufficient to prove that the transfer is wrongful

Chen clearly knows that the transfer account is owned by Shi, and the bank account transaction records provided by him can only prove that there is a transfer, but cannot prove that it is a transfer by mistake. Chen Rongping, knowing that the payee is Shi Shengen but still chooses to transfer, claims that the transfer is a" transfer by mistake ", which does not conform to the common sense, and the nature of collection is not specified in the postscript of the transfer," Ganoderm Powder ". Therefore, 500000 yuan involved in the case belongs to Shi, which is an enforceable property.
The core of the judgment of the court: Why can the claim of" wrongful transfer "not prevent the execution?

The court considers that Item 3 of Paragraph 1 of Article 25 of the Provisions of the Supreme People's Court on Several Issues Concerning the Handling of Cases of Objection to Execution and Reconsideration by People's Courts stipulates that" If an outsider raises an objection to the execution of a bank deposit enforced by the people's court, the people's court shall judge whether the objector to execution is the obligee according to the account name registered by the financial institution. "In this case, Shi A, the person subjected to execution, has an enforcement case that has not been completed, and the court's freezing of the deposit account of Shi A, the person subjected to execution, conforms to the provisions of law.For the payment of 500000 yuan remitted into the deposit account of Shi A, the person subjected to execution, as mentioned by the outsiders, Zhan and Chen, before the transfer, they know that the remitted account is the bank account under the name of the person subjected to execution, and the act does not belong to wrong remittance or wrong remittance. The court shall not support their claim that the payment belongs to them without legal basis.On this basis, the court rejected the objection request of outsiders Zhan and Chen.
Execution effect: Eventually, all the 500000 yuan of payment for goods will be used for execution, and Mr WANG's creditor's rights will be realized in full!The case was closed in February 2025.
Practice Guide for Lawyers: How to Prevent the Risk of Mistransfer?How should we deal with objections to enforcement?
Relief route for transfer of funds by mistake
Execution of Objection Procedure

This procedure is only applicable to outsiders who enjoy real rights or special creditor's rights over the subject matter of execution. As the funds in this case have been mixed with other funds after entering the account of Shi A, they are not specific and cannot return the" original object "in fact, and have no right to claim the" original object return ".Execution of ordinary claims (such as unjust enrichment in the case) cannot be excluded.
Claim of unjust enrichment in a separate action

According to the provisions of Article 14 of the Interpretation of the Supreme People's Court on Several Issues Concerning the Application of Enforcement Procedures, Zhan and Chen are unable to request the exclusion of compulsory execution on the basis of property rights on the grounds of claims for wrongful transfer of claims for unjust enrichment.
An outsider may bring a suit against the person subjected to execution in another case to demand the return of money, but he shall be compensated equally with other creditors without priority.
Special circumstances break through the possibility of" possession is ownership "
If the transfer by mistake meets the following conditions, the outsider may exclude the execution:
Special accounts for special funds: such as special accounts for migrant workers' wages and deposit accounts, etc. The funds have specific purposes and are not confused;
Account is specified: the money is not used after entering the account and is distinguished from other funds of the person subjected to execution;
Clear collection agreement: the outsider and the person subjected to execution sign a collection agreement, and the third party (the actual payee) confirms the fact of collection.
Risk prevention suggestions
Double confirmation before transfer: check the account name, account number and opening bank to avoid blind transfer only based on" kinship "or" historical transaction ".

Remarks specify the purpose: If it is collected and paid on behalf of others, it shall indicate" collect on behalf of XX "in the postscript of the transfer, and keep written agreements, chat records and other evidence.
Timely take preservation measures: immediately report to the police and apply for account freezing after discovering the wrong transfer to prevent the funds from being deducted.

en.conclusion: Whether the transfer of funds by mistake can exclude the execution lies in the determination of the ownership of funds and the nature of the rights of outsiders.The "possession is ownership" rule of currency is universal, and it is difficult for outsiders to support the exclusion of execution only by claiming "wrongful transfer".Enterprises and individuals should attach importance to account compliance management in transactions, seek professional legal assistance in a timely manner in case of disputes, and safeguard their rights and interests through accurate legal strategies.\N Follow us for more practical guidelines on enforcement objections and remedies!\N (The cases and viewpoints in this article are for practical reference, and the specific cases need to be analyzed in combination with the rules of the competent court.）

zh.title: 优先受偿权与首封债权分配规则解析：执行程序中如何“抢跑”受偿？
zh.lead: 在执行案件中，当债务人财产不足以清偿全部债务时，债权人之间的“受偿顺序之争”往往成为关键。优先受偿权如何实现？首封债权人是否享有“先到先得”的优势？本文结合法律规定与实务经验，为您厘清执行分配的底层逻辑。

blocks.text: 首封债权≠优先受偿权：法律基本原则
根据《民法典》、《民事诉讼法》及相关司法解释，优先受偿权是法律规定的特定债权人优先于其他债权人甚至优先于其他物权人受偿的权利。首封债权人适当多分配尚无明文规定。执行分配中的受偿顺位通常遵循以下规则：
法定优先权绝对优先
《民法典》规定的法定的优先权主要有抵押权、质权、留置权、建设工程价款优先全等，此外，根据不同的民事法律关系还有船舶优先权、民用航空器优先权、消费性购房优先受偿权等等，我们在此处只简单介绍民法典规定的这几种优先受偿权。
建设工程价款优先权
根据《民法典》第八百零七条规定，承包人对工程折价、拍卖款享有优先权。
担保物权（抵押权/质权/留置权）
根据《民法典》第四百一十四条、四百一十五条规定，同一财产上有多个抵押权，都登记的，按登记的时间先后确定清偿顺序，登记的优先于未登记的，都未登记的，按债权比例受偿；同一财产既设立抵押权又设立质权的，按照登记、交付的时间先后确定清偿顺序。
根据《民法典》第四百五十六条规定，同一财产上既有抵押权、质权，又有留置权的，留置权人优先受偿。
普通债权原则上按比例或查封顺序受偿
若被执行人为自然人或其他组织，其财产不足清偿全部债务时，普通债权人按债权比例分配（根据参与分配规则）。
被执行人为企业法人，若未进入破产程序，普通债权人按查封先后顺序受偿；若进入破产程序，则按破产清算规则统一分配。
首封债权人的“优势”：能否多分一杯羹？
首封债权人（普通债权人）的受偿地位需分情形讨论：
情形1：被执行人为自然人/其他组织——按比例分配
财产不足清偿全部债务时，适用参与分配制度：
优先权人直接主张优先受偿；（《民诉法司法解释》第五百零六条）。
普通债权人原则上按债权比例分配（《民诉法司法解释》第五百零八条）。
例外：部分地区允许首封债权人多分10%-20%，尽管法律未明文规定首封债权可优先受偿，但多地法院为鼓励债权人积极查找财产，允许首封普通债权人适当多分一定比例，具体规则如下：
该“奖励”仅针对普通债权，不得突破法定优先权顺位；
具体比例以当地法院规定为准。
需提供首封贡献证明（如查找财产线索的证据）。
情形2：被执行人为企业法人——首封可能“逆袭”
未进入破产程序：按查封顺序清偿（首封优先）。
依据《民诉法司法解释》第五百一十四条，若被执行人财产未进入破产程序，法定的优先受偿债权仍然优先，但普通债权按查封先后顺序受偿，此时首封债权人可以占据先发优势。
进入破产程序
依据《民诉法司法解释》第五百一十三条，若被执行人进入破产程序，执行法院应当裁定终结对该被执行人的执行。根据《企业破产法》第一百零九条和第一百一十条，对破产人的特定财产享有担保权的权利人可就该特定财产享有优先受偿权，首封优势消失，其他所有普通债权按比例清偿。
实务操作建议
首封与多分规则并用
在支持多分的地区，执行前通过财产保全或执行阶段及时查找财产线索（如银行账户、房产、股权等），抢先查封债务人核心资产；争取成为首封债权人。需要注意优先申请查封易变现、价值高无权利负担的财产，避免查封低效资产。
首封债权人人可通过提供查封时间证明、财产线索来源记录（如自行调查的书面材料、悬赏公告回执）、保全费用凭证、向执行法院书面说明首封行为对财产查控的贡献等。及时申请执行法院酌定多分比例。
规避破产程序冲击：
若债务人资不抵债，首封优势可能被破产程序打破，因此在债务人或其他债权人申请债务人破产前，尽快处置财产并分配；若其他债权人已申请破产，首封债权人也可及时申报债权并主张首封贡献，部分破产管理人可能酌情考虑。
风险警示：这些雷区千万别踩！
“躺在权利上睡觉”：若首封债权人未及时主张优先权或申请参与分配，视为放弃权利；
“误判财产线索”： 若财产已被其他优先权完全覆盖，首封财产价值不足时首封仅增加程序成本，需提前评估财产价值与优先权范围。

zh.conclusion:
执行分配是“法律规则”与“诉讼策略”的双重博弈。首封债权虽不直接等同于优先权，但通过合理运用查封顺序规则及地方司法政策，仍可显著提升受偿可能性。债权人需结合案件管辖地规则、债务人主体类型制定针对性策略，必要时建议委托专业律师介入，以最大化实现债权。
关注我们，获取更多执行实务干货！
（本文仅为实务要点提炼，具体案件需结合管辖法院规则制定策略。）

en.title: Interpretation of the rules for the distribution of priority to be repaid and the first creditor's right: How to" rush "to be repaid in the execution procedure?
en.lead: In enforcement cases, when the debtor's property is insufficient to pay off all debts, the" dispute over the order of repayment "between creditors often becomes the key.How can the priority of repayment be realized?Does the initial creditor enjoy the "first come, first served" advantage?This article combines legal provisions and practical experience to clarify the underlying logic of enforcement distribution for you.

blocks.text: The first creditor's right has the priority of repayment: the basic principle of law

According to the Civil Code, the Civil Procedure Law and relevant judicial interpretations, the priority of compensation is the right of specific creditors to be compensated in priority to other creditors or even to other property owners as stipulated by law.There is no clear provision for the appropriate distribution of the initial creditors.The order of repayment in the enforcement of an allocation generally follows the following rules:
Statutory priority absolute priority

The statutory priorities stipulated in the Civil Code mainly include mortgage, pledge, lien, priority of construction project price, etc. In addition, according to different civil legal relations, there are ship liens, civil aircraft liens, priority of compensation for consumer housing, etc. We will only briefly introduce these priority rights stipulated in the Civil Code here.
Priority of Construction Project Price
According to Article 807 of the Civil Code, the contractor shall enjoy priority over the project discount and auction money.
Security interest (mortgage/pledge/lien)

According to the provisions of Articles 414 and 415 of the Civil Code, if there are multiple mortgages on the same property and all of them are registered, the order of repayment shall be determined according to the time of registration, and the registered ones shall have priority over the unregistered ones. If none of them is registered, they shall be compensated according to the proportion of creditor's rights;If both the mortgage right and the pledge right are established for the same property, the order of repayment shall be determined according to the time of registration and delivery.
According to the provisions of Article 456 of the Civil Code, if there is both a mortgage, a pledge and a lien on the same property, the lienor shall have the priority to be repaid.
In principle, ordinary creditor's rights shall be paid in proportion or in the order of seizure

If the person subjected to execution is a natural person or other organization, and his property is insufficient to pay off all his debts, the ordinary creditor shall distribute it in proportion to his creditor's rights (according to the rules of participation in distribution).

If the person subjected to execution is an enterprise juridical person and has not entered the bankruptcy proceedings, the ordinary creditors shall be compensated in the order of seizure;If it enters the bankruptcy proceedings, it shall be distributed uniformly in accordance with the bankruptcy liquidation rules.
The" advantages "of the initial creditors: Can they have a bigger share?
The repayment status of the initial creditor (ordinary creditor) shall be discussed according to the circumstances:
Case 1: The person subjected to execution is a natural person/other organization - distributed in proportion
When the property is insufficient to pay off all the debts, the system of participation in distribution shall apply:
The priority holder directly claims priority in compensation;(Article 506 of the Judicial Interpretation of the Civil Procedure Law).
In principle, ordinary creditors shall be distributed in proportion to their creditor's rights (Article 508 of the Judicial Interpretation of the Civil Procedure Law).

Exceptions: In some areas, the initial creditor is allowed to be given an additional 10% -20%. Although the law does not expressly stipulate that the initial creditor's right may be paid in priority, the courts in many places allow the initial ordinary creditor to give an appropriate additional proportion in order to encourage creditors to actively search for property, and the specific rules are as follows:
The" reward "is only for ordinary creditor's rights and shall not exceed the legal priority order;
The specific proportion shall be subject to the provisions of the local court.
Proof of first contribution is required (such as evidence of finding property clues).
Case 2: The person subjected to execution is an enterprise juridical person - the first seal may" counterattack "
Not in bankruptcy proceedings: liquidation shall be made in the order of seizure (priority shall be given to the first seal).

According to Article 514 of the Judicial Interpretation of the Civil Procedure Law, if the property of the person subjected to execution has not entered the bankruptcy proceedings, the statutory priority creditor's rights shall still be given priority, but the ordinary creditor's rights shall be paid in the order of seizure, and the first creditor may occupy the first mover advantage.
Entering bankruptcy proceedings

According to Article 513 of the Judicial Interpretation of the Civil Procedure Law, if the person subjected to execution enters the bankruptcy proceedings, the execution court shall rule to terminate the execution of the person subjected to execution.According to Articles 109 and 110 of the Enterprise Bankruptcy Law, the obligee who has a security right over the specific property of the bankrupt may enjoy the priority of repayment with respect to the specific property, the first seal advantage disappears, and all other ordinary creditor's rights are paid off in proportion.
Suggestions for practical operation
First envelope combined with multiple rules

In areas that support multiple distribution, property clues (such as bank accounts, real estate, equity, etc.) shall be searched in time through property preservation or implementation stage before implementation to seize the core assets of the debtor first;Strive to be the first creditor.Attention should be paid to giving priority to the application for sealing up property that is easy to realize, high in value and free from encumbrances, so as to avoid sealing up inefficient assets.

The creditor of the first seal may provide proof of the time of seizure, records of the source of property clues (such as written materials for self-investigation, receipt of reward announcement), vouchers for preservation expenses, and written explanations to the enforcement court of the contribution of the first seal to the property investigation and control.The court applying for enforcement in a timely manner shall decide on the proportion of multiple points.
Avoiding the impact of insolvency proceedings:

If the debtor is insolvent, the first seal advantage may be broken by the bankruptcy proceedings, so dispose of the property and distribute it as soon as possible before the debtor or other creditors apply for bankruptcy of the debtor;If other creditors have applied for bankruptcy, the first creditor may also declare the creditor's rights in time and claim the first contribution, which may be considered by some bankruptcy administrators at their discretion.
Risk warning: Do not step on these minefields!
Sleeping on rights": If the first creditor fails to claim the priority or apply for participation in the distribution in time, it shall be deemed to have waived its rights;

Clue of Misjudgment of Property": If the property has been fully covered by other priorities, the first seal only increases the cost of the procedure when the value of the first seal property is insufficient, and the value of the property and the scope of priority need to be assessed in advance.

en.conclusion: Distribution of execution is a dual game between" legal rules "and" litigation strategies ".Although the first claim is not directly equivalent to the priority, the possibility of repayment can still be significantly enhanced through the rational use of the rules of seizure order and local judicial policies.Creditors need to formulate targeted strategies in combination with the rules of jurisdiction of the case and the types of debtors, and suggest entrusting professional lawyers to intervene when necessary to maximize the realization of creditor's rights.\N Follow us for more executive dry goods!\N (This article is only for the extraction of practical points, and specific cases need to be formulated in accordance with the rules of the competent court.）

zh.title: 跨境执行中国判决与仲裁裁决：法律框架与实务指南
zh.lead: 跨境执行是法律规则、司法互信与国际协作的综合博弈。企业应在争议发生前规划管辖条款，胜诉后结合目标国法律动态设计执行策略。专业律师团队在文件准备、互惠关系举证及程序应对中不可或缺...

blocks.text: 引言
随着全球经济一体化加速，跨境争议解决中“胜诉易、执行难”的问题日益凸显。中国法院的生效判决或仲裁裁决如何在境外获得承认与执行？本文结合中国法律、国际条约及典型国家实践，系统梳理法律依据与操作路径，为企业与法律从业者提供实务指引。
国内法依据
生效仲裁裁决
根据《民事诉讼法》第二百九十七条第二款规定，中国仲裁委员会作出的生效仲裁裁决需在境外执行时，当事人可以直接向有管辖权的外国法院申请承认和执行。
生效判决：双轨制执行路径
我国法院作出的判决目前无法依靠国际公约在其他国家得到承认和执行，根据《民事诉讼法》第二百九十七条第一款规定，中国法院的生效判决需在境外执行时，当事人可通过以下两种途径申请：
1.直接申请：当事人直接向有管辖权的外国法院申请承认和执行；
2.法院协助请求：人民法院依照中华人民共和国缔结或者参加的国际条约的规定，或者按照互惠原则，请求外国法院承认和执行。
国际条约框架：多边与双边的双重支撑
多边条约
《承认及执行外国仲裁裁决公约》（《1958年纽约公约》）
1986年12月2日我国加入1958年在纽约通过的《承认及执行外国仲裁裁决公约》（以下简称《1958年纽约公约》）该公约自1987年4月22日起对中国生效，主要内容总结如下:
《承认与执行外国民商事判决公约》（《2019年海牙公约》）
2019年7月2日，海牙国际私法大会（HCCH）第22届外交官大会的代表们签署了《承认与执行外国民商事判决公约》，中国未加入该公约，中国法院做出的生效民商事判决也不能通过该公约在缔约国法院申请承认与执行。主要内容总结如下:
双边条约
中国与39国签署民商事司法协助协定，其中34国明确包含判决相互执行条款，大致情况梳理如下：
互惠原则：从“事实”到“推定”的突破
我国与美、英、日、韩等与中国经济往来相对密切的国家，都未缔结包括互为承认和执行法院判决、裁定内容的双边司法协助协定，在无条约情况下，互惠原则是执行的核心依据。当事人仅能依据互惠原则申请、请求这些国家法院承认和执行我国法院的生效判决和裁定。各国的国内法中，通常会规定司法礼让原则或互惠原则，并根据各国的司法历史与实践，制定相关的法律。中国近年司法政策显著放宽：
事实互惠
事实互惠是指作出请求承认与执行判决的法院所属国有承认与执行被请求国法院判决的先例，被请求国对该国法院的判决也给予承认和执行以作为回报。
2009年7月22日，美国加州联邦地区法院作出裁决，承认和执行湖北省高级人民法院第（2001）鄂民四初字第1号民事判书。罗宾逊公司随后提起上诉，2011年，3月29日，美国第九巡回上诉法院作出裁定，驳回上诉，维持原判，即承认和执行中国法院的生效判决。
法律互惠
法律互惠指两国规定了大体相同的承认与执行外国法院判决的条件，并要求以国内立法形式做出互惠保证。
最高人民法院在2022年发布的《全国法院涉外商事海事审判工作座谈会会议纪要》确立“法律互惠”标准，第四十四条第一款第一项规定“根据该法院所在国的法律，人民法院作出的民商事判决可以得到该国法院的承认和执行；”互惠关系的认定不再以相关外国法院对人民法院民商事判决先行给予承认和执行为必要条件。
日本最高法院指出，《日本民事诉讼法》118条第4款规定的“互惠的存在”是指，“原审国对外国判决承认和执行的要求与日本法中所规定的要求没有实质上的区别，从而使同类的日本法院判决也能在原审国有效”。日本采取这种宽泛的互惠认定标准，促进了与判决作出国之间互惠关系的建立，有些日本学者也据此建议在立法中取消互惠的规定。
推定互惠

推定互惠是指通过两国法院达成的互惠谅解或者共识或者外交互惠承诺等事实推定两国存有互惠关系。2017年6月，在最高人民法院举办的第二届中国-东盟大法官会议上通过了《南宁声明》。声明强调，“与会各国法院将……考虑适当促进各国民商事判决的相互承认和执行。尚未缔结有关外国民商事判决承认和执行国际条约的国家，在承认与执行对方国家民商事判决的司法程序中，如对方国家的法院不存在以互惠为理由拒绝承认和执行本国民商事判决的先例，在本国国内法允许的范围内，即可推定与对方国家之间存在互惠关系。”从而将互惠原则在司法实践中的适用从“事实互惠”标准向“推定互惠”标准迈进，有利于中国与没有条约基础的国家之间开展相关司法协助工作。
2024年：南宁中院首次以“推定互惠”执行泰国判决，释放积极信号。但在实践中，部分国家仍要求事实互惠，需个案举证对方存在执行先例。

references: 《中华人民共和国民事诉讼法》
《全国法院涉外商事海事审判工作座谈会会议纪要》
《承认及执行外国仲裁裁决公约》
《承认与执行外国民商事判决公约》
《关于从国外调取民事或商事证据的公约》
《【研讨会议题三主题发言】马培德：中国和德国在承认和执行外国判决方面的跨境司法合作》
《【研讨会议题四主题发言】高晓力：加强民商事国际司法协助 服务国际商事争议解决》
zh.subtitle: ——从国内立法到国际条约及互惠原则的全面解析
zh.conclusion: 跨境执行是法律规则、司法互信与国际协作的综合博弈。企业应在争议发生前规划管辖条款，胜诉后结合目标国法律动态设计执行策略。专业律师团队在文件准备、互惠关系举证及程序应对中不可或缺。

en.title: Cross-border Enforcement of Chinese Judgments and Arbitral Awards: Legal Framework and Practical Guidelines
en.lead: Cross-border enforcement is a comprehensive game of legal rules, judicial mutual trust and international cooperation.Enterprises should plan jurisdiction clauses before disputes occur, and design implementation strategies dynamically in light of the laws of the target country after winning the lawsuit.A team of professional lawyers is indispensable in the preparation of documents, the proof of reciprocity and procedural response..
en.subtitle: - Comprehensive analysis from domestic legislation to international treaties and the principle of reciprocity

blocks.text: Introduction

With the acceleration of global economic integration, the problem of" victory in litigation and difficulty in enforcement "in cross-border dispute settlement has become increasingly prominent.How can an effective judgment or arbitral award of a Chinese court be recognized and enforced abroad?Based on Chinese laws, international treaties and typical state practices, this article systematically combs the legal basis and operation path, and provides practical guidance for enterprises and legal practitioners.
Basis of domestic law
Effective arbitral award

In accordance with the provisions of the second paragraph of Article 297 of the Civil Procedure Law, if an effective arbitral award made by the China Arbitration Commission needs to be enforced abroad, the parties may directly apply to the competent foreign court for recognition and enforcement.
