Effective judgment: dual track implementation path

At present, judgments made by Chinese courts cannot be recognized and enforced in other countries by relying on international conventions. According to the first paragraph of Article 297 of the Civil Procedure Law, when an effective judgment of a Chinese court needs to be executed abroad, the parties concerned may apply through the following two channels:
1. Direct application: a party directly applies to a foreign court with jurisdiction for recognition and enforcement;

2. Request for assistance of the court: The people's court shall, in accordance with the provisions of the international treaties concluded or acceded to by the People's Republic of China, or in accordance with the principle of reciprocity, request the foreign court to recognize and enforce them.
International treaty framework: dual support of multilateral and bilateral
Multilateral treaties
Convention on the Recognition and Enforcement of Foreign Arbitral Awards (New York Convention 1958)

On December 2, 1986, China acceded to the Convention on the Recognition and Enforcement of Foreign Arbitral Awards adopted in New York in 1958 (hereinafter referred to as the" New York Convention 1958 "), which shall enter into force for China on April 22, 1987. The main contents are summarized as follows:
Convention on the Recognition and Enforcement of Foreign Civil and Commercial Judgments (Hague Convention 2019)

On July 2, 2019, representatives of the 22nd Diplomatic Congress of the Hague Conference on Private International Law (HCCH) signed the Convention on the Recognition and Enforcement of Foreign Civil and Commercial Judgments. China has not acceded to the Convention, and the effective civil and commercial judgments made by Chinese courts cannot be applied for recognition and enforcement in the courts of the contracting States through the Convention.The main contents are summarized as follows:
bilateral treaty

China and 39 countries have signed agreements on judicial assistance in civil and commercial matters, including 34 countries that explicitly include provisions on mutual enforcement of judgments. The general situation is summarized as follows:
The principle of reciprocity: a breakthrough from" fact "to" presumption "

China has not concluded bilateral judicial assistance agreements with the United States, Britain, Japan, South Korea and other countries with relatively close economic exchanges with China, including mutual recognition and enforcement of court judgments and rulings. In the absence of treaties, the principle of reciprocity is the core basis for enforcement.The parties concerned can only apply for and request the courts of these countries to recognize and enforce the effective judgments and rulings of the courts of our country on the basis of the principle of reciprocity.The principle of judicial courtesy or reciprocity is usually stipulated in the domestic laws of various countries, and relevant laws are formulated according to the judicial history and practice of various countries.China's judicial policies have been significantly relaxed in recent years:
factual reciprocity

factual reciprocity means that the State to which the court making the request for recognition and enforcement of the judgment belongs has a precedent for recognition and enforcement of the judgment of the court of the requested State, and the requested State has also recognized and enforced the judgment of the court of that State in return.

On July 22, 2009, the Federal District Court of California of the United States made a ruling to recognize and enforce the Civil Judgment No. 1 [2001] of the Higher People's Court of Hubei Province.Robinson subsequently filed an appeal, and on March 29, 2011, the United States Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit ruled to reject the appeal and uphold the original judgment, that is, to recognize and enforce the effective judgment of the Chinese court.
Legal reciprocity

Legal reciprocity means that both countries stipulate substantially the same conditions for recognition and enforcement of foreign court judgments and require reciprocal assurances in the form of domestic legislation.

The Minutes of the National Symposium on Foreign-related Commercial Maritime Trials issued by the Supreme People's Court in 2022 establish the standard of" legal reciprocity ". Paragraph 1 (1) of Article 44 stipulates that" according to the law of the country where the court is located, the civil and commercial judgment made by the people's court may be recognized and enforced by the court of the country where the court is located; "The determination of reciprocity no longer requires the relevant foreign court to recognize and enforce the civil and commercial judgment of the people's court in advance.

The Supreme Court of Japan has pointed out that the" existence of reciprocity "stipulated in Article 118, paragraph 4, of the Japanese Civil Procedure Law means that" there is no substantial difference between the requirements for recognition and enforcement of foreign judgments in the original trial country and those stipulated in the Japanese law, so that similar Japanese court judgments can also be valid in the original trial country ".The adoption of such a broad standard of reciprocity in Japan has promoted the establishment of a mutually beneficial relationship with the country making the judgment, and some Japanese scholars have accordingly suggested that the provisions on reciprocity be abolished in legislation.
presumed reciprocity

Presumptive reciprocity refers to the presumption of reciprocity between the two countries through such facts as mutual understanding or consensus reached by the courts of the two countries or diplomatic promise of reciprocity.In June 2017, the Nanning Declaration was adopted at the Second China ASEAN Judges' Meeting held by the Supreme People's Court.The statement emphasizes that "the courts of the participating countries will... consider appropriate promotion of mutual recognition and enforcement of civil and commercial judgments. A country that has not yet concluded an international treaty on the recognition and enforcement of foreign civil and commercial judgments may presume that there is a reciprocity relationship with the other country to the extent permitted by its domestic law if the courts of the other country do not have a precedent of refusing to recognize and enforce their own civil and commercial judgments on the grounds of reciprocity in the judicial proceedings for the recognition and enforcement of the civil and commercial judgments of the other country." Thus, the application of the principle of reciprocity in judicial practice is advanced from the "factual reciprocity" standard to the "presumed reciprocity" standard, which is conducive to the relevant judicial assistance work between China and countries without a treaty basis.

2024: Nanning Intermediate People's Court executes the Thai judgment with" presumed reciprocity "for the first time, releasing positive signals.However, in practice, some countries still require reciprocity of facts and need to prove the existence of enforcement precedents in the other party by individual cases.

en.conclusion: Cross-border enforcement is a comprehensive game of legal rules, judicial mutual trust and international cooperation.Enterprises should plan jurisdiction clauses before disputes occur, and design implementation strategies dynamically in light of the laws of the target country after winning the lawsuit.A team of professional lawyers is indispensable in the preparation of documents, the proof of reciprocity and procedural response.

zh.title: 外国生效仲裁裁决在中国的承认与执行实务指南
zh.lead: 中国法院近年来通过统一司法解释、扩大互惠认定范围，显著提升了外国仲裁裁决的执行效率。对于缔约国裁决，中国已形成成熟的审查流程...

blocks.text: 《纽约公约》缔约国：依国际条约直接申请
法律依据
中国于1986年加入《承认及执行外国仲裁裁决公约》（《1958年纽约公约》），该公约自1987年4月22日起对中国生效，并作出两项保留：
互惠保留：仅承认和执行在另一缔约国领土内作出的裁决；
商事保留：仅适用于契约性或非契约性商事争议（如合同纠纷、侵权赔偿、投资争议等），排除东道国与外国投资者间的投资争端。
申请流程
管辖法院：（《中华人民共和国民事诉讼法》第三百零四条）
地域管辖：（1）被执行人住所地法院；（2）被执行人财产所在地法院；（3）申请人住所地法院；（4）与裁决的纠纷有适当联系的地点法院
级别管辖：中级人民法院
提交材料
裁决书正本或经认证的副本；
契约中的仲裁条款或仲裁协议的正本或经认证的副本；
中文译本。译本应由公设或宣誓之翻译员或外交或领事人员认证之。
申请执行期间
申请执行的期间为二年。申请执行时效的中止、中断，适用法律有关诉讼时效中止、中断的规定。
仲裁保全（《全国法院涉外商事海事审判工作座谈会会议纪要》第一百零八条）
当事人向人民法院申请承认和执行外国仲裁裁决，人民法院受理申请后，当事人申请财产保全的，人民法院可以参照民事诉讼法及相关司法解释的规定执行。申请人应当提供担保，不提供担保的，裁定驳回申请。
审查标准
依被申请人主张审查：
（1）当事人因法律无行为能力导致仲裁协议无效，或协议依约定的准据法系属无效，或未指明以何法律为准时，依裁决地所在国法律系属无效者；
（2）被申请人未收到指派仲裁员或仲裁程序的适当通知，或因其他原因未能陈述意见；
（3）裁决处理了未约定仲裁的事项，但交付仲裁事项之决定可与未交付仲裁之事项划分时，裁决中关于交付仲裁事项之决定部分得予承认及执行；
（4）仲裁庭组成或程序不符合当事人约定或仲裁地法律。
（5）裁决尚未生效，或被地所在国或裁决所依据法律之国家之主管机关撤销或停止执行者。
依职权审查：
（1）依该国法律，争议事项系不能以仲裁解决者；
（2）承认或执行裁决有违我国公共政策者。
《全国法院涉外商事海事审判工作座谈会会议纪要》第一百零八条规定，人民法院生效裁定已经认定当事人之间的仲裁协议不成立、无效、失效或者不可执行，承认和执行该裁决将与人民法院生效裁定相冲突的，应当认定构成规定的违反我国公共政策的情形。

不过，根据我国承认与执行外国生效仲裁裁决的司法实践来看，我国对公共政策抗辩持审慎态度，该抗辩理由获得支持的难度较大，目前，仅在（2008）民四他字第11号案中，最高人民法院认为在中国有关法院就济南永宁制药股份有限公司与合资公司济南——海慕法姆制药有限公司之间的租赁合同纠纷裁定对合资公司的财产进行保全并作出判决的情况下，国际商会仲裁院再对济南永宁制药股份有限公司与合资公司济南——海慕法姆制药有限公司之间的租赁合同纠纷进行审理并裁决，侵犯了中国的司法主权和中国法院的司法管辖权。依据《1958年纽约公约》第五条第一款（丙）项和第二款（乙）项之规定，拒绝承认和执行国际商会仲裁院第13464/MS/JB/JEM号仲裁裁决。
不予承认与执行外国仲裁机构的裁决的层报审查：

根据《最高人民法院关于人民法院处理与涉外仲裁及外国仲裁事项有关问题的通知》凡一方当事人向人民法院申请承认和执行外国仲裁机构的裁决，如果人民法院认为我国涉外仲裁机构裁决符合不予承认与执行的情形的，在裁定不予执行或者拒绝承认和执行之前，必须报请本辖区所属高级人民法院进行审查；如果高级人民法院同意不予执行或者拒绝承认和执行，应将其审查意见报最高人民法院。待最高人民法院答复后，方可裁定不予执行或者拒绝承认和执行。
暂缓执行与担保
当被申请执行人已向仲裁地国法院申请撤销或中止裁决时，受理执行申请的法院可暂缓执行，并可依申请执行方的请求，要求被申请执行人提供适当担保。
临时仲裁庭仲裁的承认与执行问题——仲裁地标准认定
根据《最高人民法院关于适用<中华人民共和国民事诉讼法>的解释》（2022修正）的相关规定，临时仲裁庭在中华人民共和国领域外作出的仲裁裁决，当事人可以直接向被执行人住所地或者其财产所在地的中级人民法院申请执行，具体规则参照外国仲裁裁决在我国法院申请承认与执行的相关规则。

而临时仲裁庭在中华人民共和国领域内作出的仲裁裁决，并不属于“在另一缔约国领土内”做出的仲裁裁决，因此无法适用《1958年纽约公约》的规定，予以承认和执行。事实上，这是由于中国国内法上对仲裁裁决国籍的认定标准不明确，且立法上突出强调“仲裁机构”，而与国际条约和国际仲裁实践中主要依“仲裁地”作为认定仲裁裁决国籍的标准不一致造成的。对此，最高人民法院的高晓力法官倾向于在司法实践中依据“仲裁地标准”认定仲裁裁决的国籍，即在个案中可以认为外国仲裁机构在中国境内作出的仲裁裁决不是外国仲裁裁决，而是中国的涉外仲裁裁决，进而避免上述适用法律的矛盾。
典型案例
（2016）辽02协外认2号案
新和海事公司与大连新和公司、高德胜因《合作协议》起纠纷，前者依约在新加坡国际仲裁中心（SIAC）申请仲裁并获支持，而后向大连中院申请承认与执行该裁决。
被申请人提出多项抗辩理由。其一，主张仲裁协议无效，称高德胜签订协议时不知晓仲裁条款，双方就仲裁条款未达成合意。其二，提出承认和执行裁决与中国公共政策抵触，包括违背诚实信用原则、与《商标法》冲突以及与已生效行政裁决书相悖。其三，指出仲裁裁决有不可执行内容，涉案商标持有人是大连新和公司，裁决却要求高德胜个人也履行义务，且被执行主体和给付内容不明确。
大连中院经审查认为，被申请人不否认《合作协议》及签字盖章真实性，协议明确约定仲裁条款及适用新加坡法律，对仲裁条款效力审查应依新加坡法律。仲裁裁决中已认定被申请人未提供证明协议无效的重要事实证据，被申请人在本案中也未提交新证据，故其主张不成立。
《纽约公约》规定的违反公共政策指严重违反中国法律基本原则等情形，本案并不存在。高德胜作为公司董事长，签订协议时应尽审慎注意义务，其主张不知晓仲裁条款有悖诚实信用。且新和海事公司向商标局申请与向 SIAC 申请仲裁不冲突，仲裁裁决未对中国商标行政管理机关行为及涉案商标合法性、有效性作出裁判，承认和执行该裁决不会与《商标法》及已生效行政裁决书相悖，也不损害中国根本社会公共利益。
依相关规定，裁决是否具有可执行性不属于《纽约公约》规定的拒绝承认情形。涉案商标虽登记在大连新和公司名下，但高德胜作为法定代表人在商标处理上有配合义务，裁决被执行主体、给付内容明确，具有可执行性。

blocks.content: 最终，大连中院认定被申请人理由均不构成《纽约公约》规定的拒绝承认及执行情形，对SIAC作出的（2015）裁字第49号仲裁裁决承认其效力并予以执行 。该案例体现出中国法院在审查承认和执行外国仲裁裁决时，严格依据《纽约公约》及相关法律规定，准确判断各项抗辩理由，切实保障当事人合法权益与仲裁裁决的有效执行。

blocks.text: （2013）锡商外仲审字第0009号案
申请人艾伦宝公司请求中国法院承认与执行国际棉花协会（ICA）就395080号合同作出的仲裁裁决，争议核心在于双方是否达成有效的仲裁协议，尤其是被申请人倪家巷公司是否通过代理人张永忠签署了包含仲裁条款的合同。法院指出，仲裁协议是否成立是仲裁协议效力的前提，属于《纽约公约》第五条第一款（甲）项的审查范围。
根据英国法（仲裁地法律），仲裁协议成立需双方明确合意。本案中，申请人未能证明张永忠有权代表倪家巷公司签署合同或达成仲裁合意。
申请人提供的合同仅有张永忠签字，但未加盖倪家巷公司公章，且张永忠否认签字真实性。法院要求申请人进一步举证签字真实性，但申请人未申请笔迹鉴定，导致合同真实性无法确认。
历史交易显示，倪家巷公司过往合同均需签字并加盖公章，而本案仅以传真形式签署，与惯例不符，加重了对仲裁合意的怀疑。
根据英国代理法，代理人需有实际授权（明示或默示）或表面授权。法院认为，张永忠名片同时显示倪家巷公司与关联公司抬头，申请人未核实其代理权限，且未要求加盖公章，无法证明其有合理理由相信张永忠的授权。倪家巷公司明确表示不追认张永忠的签字行为，进一步削弱了仲裁协议的有效性。
最终，无锡市中级人民法院最终以仲裁协议未成立为由拒绝承认与执行。
双边司法协助缔约国：依条约特殊规则补充适用
法律基础
中国与39个国家签署了双边民商事司法协助条约，除朝鲜外，其他国家均为《1958年纽约公约》缔约国，多数条约直接指向适用《1958年纽约公约》，也有国家在条约中约定独立规则。
申请路径
优先适用双边条约：若条约对执行程序有特殊规定（如更宽松的审查标准），优先适用；
与公约并行：若无特殊规则，仍按《纽约公约》执行。
中国与新加坡签订的《中华人民共和国和新加坡共和国关于民事和商事司法协助的条约》中明确将仲裁裁决纳入承认范围，根据《1958年纽约公约》相互承认与执行在对方境内作出的仲裁裁决。相互提供缔约双方有关民事和商事的法律及民事和商事诉讼方面司法实践的资料。同时排除被请求方对诉讼事由拥有专属管辖权或其国内法不承认对此具有诉讼权为由拒绝执行。这种制度衔接既符合国际商事仲裁惯例，又通过条约形式强化了执行效力。
非公约国家：基于互惠原则审慎推进
法律框架
对于未加入《1958年纽约公约》且无双边条约的国家（如部分非洲国家），中国法院依据《民事诉讼法》第283条，按互惠原则审查执行申请。
互惠认定标准
•传统“事实互惠”：需证明对方国家曾承认中国裁决；
•新型“推定互惠”：最高人民法院近年倾向于宽松认定，只要无证据证明对方拒绝中国裁决，即可推定互惠存在
实务难点
举证责任：申请人需提供对方国家法律或司法实践支持互惠的证据；
风险预判：中国法院尚未出现依据互惠原则执行非公约国家裁决的公开案例，程序周期可能较长。

references: 《全国法院涉外商事海事审判工作座谈会会议纪要》
《最高人民法院关于审理仲裁司法审查案件若干问题的规定》
《最高人民法院关于执行我国加入的<承认及执行外国仲裁裁决公约>的通知》
《最高人民法院关于适用<中华人民共和国民事诉讼法>的解释》（2022修正）
《最高人民法院关于人民法院处理与涉外仲裁及外国仲裁事项有关问题的通知》（2008修订）
高晓力：《谈中国法院承认和执行外国仲裁裁决的积极实践》，来源《法律适用》
复旦大学：“一带一路”国家法律研究所《“一带一路”投资的国际法律规则研究》
zh.subtitle: ——基于《纽约公约》及中国司法实践的路径解析
zh.conclusion: 中国法院近年来通过统一司法解释、扩大互惠认定范围，显著提升了外国仲裁裁决的执行效率。对于缔约国裁决，中国已形成成熟的审查流程；对于非公约国家，随着“一带一路”倡议推进，互惠原则的灵活适用将逐步破冰。企业跨境争议解决时，建议优先选择《1958年纽约公约》缔约国仲裁机构，并提前规划执行路径。

en.title: Practical Guidelines for the Recognition and Enforcement of Foreign Effective Arbitral Awards in China
en.lead: In recent years, Chinese courts have significantly improved the efficiency of enforcement of foreign arbitral awards by unifying judicial interpretations and expanding the scope of reciprocity determination.China has developed a mature review process for the ruling of the contracting parties..
en.subtitle: - Analysis of the path based on the New York Convention and China's judicial practice

blocks.text: Parties to the New York Convention: direct application under international treaties
Legal basis

China acceded to the Convention on the Recognition and Enforcement of Foreign Arbitral Awards (New York Convention 1958) in 1986, which entered into force for China as of April 22, 1987, and made two reservations:
Reciprocal reservation: recognition and enforcement only of an award made in the territory of another Contracting State;

Commercial reservation: only applicable to contractual or non-contractual commercial disputes (such as contract disputes, tort compensation, investment disputes, etc.), excluding investment disputes between the host country and foreign investors.
Application Process
The competent court: (Article 304 of the Civil Procedure Law of the People's Republic of China)

Territorial jurisdiction: (1) the court of the place where the person subjected to execution has his domicile;(2) The court of the place where the property of the person subjected to execution is located;(3) The court of the place where the applicant has his domicile;(4) Place of appropriate connection with the dispute adjudicated Court
Jurisdiction at different levels: intermediate people's courts
Submittals
The original or certified copy of the award;
The original or certified copy of the arbitration clause or arbitration agreement in the deed;
Chinese translation.The translation shall be authenticated by a public or sworn interpreter or diplomatic or consular officer.
Period of application for execution

The period of application for enforcement shall be two years.The provisions of the law on the suspension or interruption of the limitation of action shall apply to the suspension or interruption of the limitation of application for execution.
Arbitration preservation (Article 108 of the Minutes of the National Symposium on Foreign-related Commercial Maritime Trials by Courts)

If a party applies to the people's court for recognition and enforcement of a foreign arbitral award, and the people's court accepts the application, and the party applies for property preservation, the people's court may enforce it with reference to the provisions of the Civil Procedure Law and relevant judicial interpretations.The applicant shall provide guarantee, and if no guarantee is provided, the application shall be rejected by a ruling.
Review Criteria
Examination as claimed by the respondent:

(1) The arbitration agreement is invalid due to the legal incapacity of the parties, or the agreement is invalid according to the agreed applicable law system, or the law of the country where the award is made is invalid according to the law of the country where the award is made without specifying the law;
(2) The respondent has not received appropriate notice of the appointment of an arbitrator or of the arbitration proceedings, or has failed to state its opinion for other reasons;

(3) If the award deals with matters that have not been agreed upon for arbitration, but the decision on the matters to be submitted for arbitration may be divided from the matters that have not been submitted for arbitration, the part of the award on the matters to be submitted for arbitration may be recognized and enforced;
(4) The composition or procedure of the arbitration tribunal is inconsistent with the agreement of the parties or the law of the place of arbitration.
(5) The award has not yet taken effect, or its enforcement has been revoked or suspended by the competent authority of the country where it is located or of the country on which the award is based.
Review ex officio:
(1) The matter in dispute cannot be settled by arbitration in accordance with the law of that State;
(2) The recognition or enforcement of an award is contrary to the public policy of our country.

Article 108 of the Minutes of the National Symposium on Foreign-related Commercial Maritime Trial of Courts stipulates that if the effective ruling of the people's court has determined that the arbitration agreement between the parties is untenable, invalid, invalid or unenforceable, and the recognition and enforcement of the award will conflict with the effective ruling of the people's court, it shall be determined that it constitutes a violation of China's public policy.

However, according to China's judicial practice of recognizing and enforcing foreign effective arbitral awards, China adopts a prudent attitude towards public policy defenses, and it is difficult to obtain support for such defenses. Currently, only in the case of (2008) Min Si Ta Zi No. 11, the Supreme People's Court held that under the circumstances where the relevant Chinese courts had ruled to preserve the property of the joint venture company and rendered a judgment in the lease contract dispute between Jinan Yongning Pharmaceutical Co., Ltd. and the joint venture company Jinan-Haimofam Pharmaceutical Co., Ltd., the Arbitration Court of the International Chamber of Commerce further adjudicated and ruled on the lease contract dispute between Jinan Yongning Pharmaceutical Co., Ltd. and the joint venture company Jinan-Haimofam Pharmaceutical Co., Ltd., which infringed upon China's judicial sovereignty and the judicial jurisdiction of Chinese courts. In accordance with the provisions of Article 5, paragraphs 1 (c) and 2 (b), of the 1958 New York Convention, it refused to recognize and enforce the arbitral award No. 13464/MS/JB/JEM of the Arbitration Court of the International Chamber of Commerce.
Review of the Inadmissibility to Recognition and Enforcement of Awards of Foreign Arbitral Institutions:

In accordance with the Notice of the Supreme People's Court on Issues Concerning the Handling of Foreign-related Arbitration and Foreign Arbitration Matters by People's Courts, if one party applies to the people's court for recognition and enforcement of the award of a foreign arbitration institution, if the people's court considers that the award of a foreign-related arbitration institution in China conforms to the circumstances of non-recognition and enforcement, it must report to the higher people's court under its jurisdiction for examination before the ruling is not enforced or the recognition and enforcement are refused;If the Higher People's Court agrees not to enforce or refuses to recognize and enforce, it shall submit its examination opinions to the Supreme People's Court.Only after the Supreme People's Court has given a reply may it rule not to enforce or refuse to recognize and enforce it.
Suspension of Enforcement and Guarantee

When the person against whom enforcement is sought has applied to the court of the country of arbitration for revocation or suspension of the award, the court accepting the application for enforcement may suspend enforcement and, at the request of the party against whom enforcement is sought, require the person against whom enforcement is sought to provide appropriate security.
Recognition and enforcement of arbitration by an interim arbitral tribunal - Standard determination of the place of arbitration

In accordance with the relevant provisions of the Interpretation of the Supreme People's Court on the Application of the Civil Procedure Law of the People's Republic of China (2022 Revision), the parties may directly apply for enforcement of an arbitral award made by a provisional arbitral tribunal outside the territory of the People's Republic of China to the intermediate people's court of the place where the person subjected to enforcement has his domicile or where his property is located. The specific rules shall refer to the relevant rules on the application for recognition and enforcement of foreign arbitral awards in Chinese courts.

The arbitral award made by the provisional arbitral tribunal within the territory of the People's Republic of China does not belong to an arbitral award made" within the territory of another contracting state ", and therefore cannot be recognized and enforced in accordance with the provisions of the 1958 New York Convention.As a matter of fact, this is due to the unclear criteria for determining the nationality of arbitral awards in China's domestic law, and the prominent emphasis on "arbitration institutions" in legislation, which is inconsistent with the criteria for determining the nationality of arbitral awards mainly based on "arbitration place" in international treaties and international arbitration practice.For this reason, Judge Gao Xiaoli of the Supreme People's Court tends to determine the nationality of an arbitral award in judicial practice according to the "standard of the place of arbitration", that is, in a case, it can be considered that the arbitral award made by a foreign arbitral institution within the territory of China is not a foreign arbitral award, but a foreign-related arbitral award of China, so as to avoid the above-mentioned contradictions in the application of law.
Model Case
(2016) Liao 02 Xie Wai Ren No. 2 Case

If a dispute arises between Xinhe Maritime Company and Dalian Xinhe Company and Gao Desheng due to the Cooperation Agreement, the former shall apply for arbitration at the Singapore International Arbitration Center (SIAC) in accordance with the agreement and obtain support, and then apply to the Dalian Intermediate Court for recognition and enforcement of the award.

The respondent raises several defenses.First, it claims that the arbitration agreement is invalid, saying that Gao Desheng did not know the arbitration clause when signing the agreement, and the two parties did not reach an agreement on the arbitration clause.Secondly, it is proposed that recognition and enforcement of rulings conflict with Chinese public policies, including violation of the principle of good faith, conflict with the Trademark Law and conflict with effective administrative rulings.Thirdly, it is pointed out that the arbitral award has unenforceable contents, and the trademark holder involved is Dalian Xinhe Company, but the award requires Gao Desheng himself to perform his obligations, and the subject of enforcement and the content of payment are not clear.

Upon examination, the Dalian Intermediate People's Court holds that the Respondent does not deny the authenticity of the Cooperation Agreement and its signature and seal, that the agreement clearly stipulates the arbitration clause and the application of Singapore law, and that the validity of the arbitration clause shall be examined in accordance with Singapore law.The arbitration award has determined that the respondent has not provided important factual evidence to prove the invalidity of the agreement, and the respondent has not submitted new evidence in the case, so its claim is untenable.

The violation of public policy stipulated in the New York Convention refers to a serious violation of the basic principles of Chinese law, and the case does not exist.As the chairman of the board of directors of the company, Gao Desheng shall exercise due diligence when signing the agreement, and his claim that he does not know that the arbitration clause is contrary to good faith.Moreover, Xinhe Maritime Company's application to the Trademark Office does not conflict with its application to the SIAC for arbitration. The arbitration award does not adjudicate on the acts of the Chinese trademark administrative organ and the legality and validity of the trademark involved in the case, and the recognition and enforcement of the award will not conflict with the Trademark Law and the effective administrative award, nor will it harm the fundamental social and public interests of China.

Whether an award is enforceable in accordance with the relevant provisions does not fall within the circumstances of refusal of recognition under the New York Convention.Although the trademark involved in the case is registered in the name of Dalian Xinhe Company, as the legal representative, Gao Desheng has the obligation to cooperate in the handling of the trademark, and the subject and content of the award are clear and enforceable.

blocks.content: Finally, the Dalian Intermediate People's Court determined that the reasons of the respondent do not constitute a refusal of recognition and enforcement under the New York Convention, and recognized the validity of the arbitral award No. 49 (2015) made by the SIAC and enforced it.The case reflects that when examining, recognizing and enforcing foreign arbitral awards, the Chinese court shall, in strict accordance with the provisions of the New York Convention and relevant laws, accurately judge the reasons for defense, and effectively safeguard the legitimate rights and interests of the parties and the effective enforcement of the arbitral award.

blocks.text: Case No. 0009 [2013] of the Supreme People's Court

The Claimant Allen Bow Company requests the Chinese Court to recognize and enforce the arbitral award made by the International Cotton Association (ICA) in respect of Contract No. 395080. The core of the dispute is whether the two parties have reached a valid arbitration agreement, especially whether the Respondent Ni Jiaxiang Company has signed a contract containing an arbitration clause through its agent Zhang Yongzhong.The court pointed out that whether an arbitration agreement is established is a prerequisite for the validity of the arbitration agreement, which falls within the scope of examination of Article 5, paragraph 1 (a), of the New York Convention.

According to the English Law (Law of the Place of Arbitration), the formation of an arbitration agreement requires the express consent of both parties.In this case, the applicant failed to prove that Zhang Yongzhong has the right to sign a contract or reach an arbitration agreement on behalf of Ni Jiaxiang Company.

The contract provided by the applicant is only signed by Zhang Yongzhong, but it is not stamped with the official seal of Ni Jiaxiang Company, and Zhang Yongzhong denies the authenticity of the signature.The court required the applicant to further prove the authenticity of the signature, but the applicant did not apply for handwriting appraisal, resulting in the failure to confirm the authenticity of the contract.

Historical transactions show that all previous contracts of Nijiaxiang Company need to be signed and sealed, but the case is only signed by fax, which is inconsistent with the practice and aggravates the doubt on the arbitration agreement.

Under the British Agency Law, an agent is required to have actual authority (express or implied) or apparent authority.The court held that the name card of Zhang Yongzhong showed the title of Ni Jiaxiang Company and the affiliated company at the same time, and the applicant did not verify his agency authority and did not require the affixing of official seal, so it could not prove that he had reasonable reasons to believe in the authorization of Zhang Yongzhong.Ni Jiaxiang Company clearly indicated that it would not ratify the signature of Zhang Yongzhong, which further weakened the validity of the arbitration agreement.
Finally, the Wuxi Intermediate People's Court finally refused to recognize and enforce the arbitration agreement on the grounds that it was not established.
Parties to bilateral mutual legal assistance: supplementary application in accordance with the special rules of the treaty
Legal basis

China has signed bilateral treaties on mutual legal assistance in civil and commercial matters with 39 countries. Except North Korea, all other countries are parties to the 1958 New York Convention. Most treaties directly refer to the application of the 1958 New York Convention, and some countries have agreed on independent rules in the treaties.
Application path

Priority in the application of bilateral treaties: if the treaty has special provisions on the implementation procedure (such as more lenient review standards), priority shall be given to the application;
Parallel with the Convention: if there are no special rules, they shall still be implemented in accordance with the New York Convention.

The Treaty between the People's Republic of China and the Republic of Singapore on Judicial Assistance in Civil and Commercial Matters concluded between China and Singapore expressly includes arbitral awards in the scope of recognition, and mutual recognition and enforcement of arbitral awards made in the territory of the other party in accordance with the New York Convention 1958.To provide each other with information on the laws and judicial practice of the Contracting Parties in civil and commercial matters.At the same time, it is excluded that the Requested Party has exclusive jurisdiction over the cause of action or refuses to enforce on the grounds that its domestic law does not recognize its right of action.This system not only conforms to the practice of international commercial arbitration, but also strengthens the enforcement effect in the form of treaties.
Non-Convention countries: prudent promotion based on the principle of reciprocity
Legal Framework

For countries that have not acceded to the 1958 New York Convention and do not have bilateral treaties (such as some African countries), the Chinese court shall examine the application for enforcement on the principle of reciprocity in accordance with Article 283 of the Civil Procedure Law.
Criteria for determining reciprocity
• Traditional" factual reciprocity ": it is necessary to prove that the other country has recognized the Chinese award;

•New "presumed reciprocity": In recent years, the Supreme People's Court tends to adopt a lenient standard of determination. As long as there is no evidence proving that the other party has refused to recognize Chinese awards, reciprocity may be presumed to exist.
Practical difficulties
Burden of proof: The applicant shall provide evidence that the laws or judicial practices of the other party's country support reciprocity;

Risk pre-judgment: Chinese courts have not yet seen public cases of enforcement of non-convention country awards on the basis of the principle of reciprocity, and the procedure cycle may be relatively long.

en.conclusion: In recent years, Chinese courts have significantly improved the efficiency of enforcement of foreign arbitral awards by unifying judicial interpretations and expanding the scope of reciprocity determination.China has formed a mature review process for the ruling of the contracting parties;For non-convention countries, with the promotion of the Belt and Road Initiative, the flexible application of the principle of reciprocity will gradually break the ice.When settling cross-border disputes, enterprises are suggested to give priority to arbitration institutions in the States parties to the 1958 New York Convention and plan the implementation path in advance.

zh.title: 一人有限公司股东的股东是否应当承担连带责任？司法实践中的不同观点与裁判思路
zh.lead: 在公司法领域，一人有限公司股东的股东（一人有限公司的股东也是一人有限公司）是否应当对公司债务承担连带责任，一直是备受关注且存在争议的法律问题。这一问题不仅关系到债权人的利益实现，也影响着公司股东责任的界定以及市场交易秩序的稳定。本文将结合相关法律法规及司法实践案例，深入探讨这一问题。

blocks.text: 法律规定奠定责任基础
《中华人民共和国公司法》第二十三条明确规定，明确一人公司股东若不能证明公司财产独立，需对公司债务承担连带责任，这为追究一人有限公司股东的责任提供了法律依据。但未提及能否追究“股东的股东”责任。
《最高人民法院关于民事执行中变更、追加当事人若干问题的规定》（2020修正）第20条进一步明确，作为被执行人的一人有限责任公司，财产不足以清偿生效法律文书确定的债务，股东不能证明公司财产独立于自己的财产，申请执行人申请变更、追加该股东为被执行人，对公司债务承担连带责任的，人民法院应予支持。这些规定构建了一人有限公司股东连带责任的基本法律框架。
司法观点分歧显著
上海一中院与上海市法学会商法学研究会联合举办的“新旧公司法衔接适用问题”研讨会指出，若将问题一般化为“债权人能否追究股东的股东的出资责任”，答案是否定的。其理由在于，股东认缴的出资构成公司的责任财产，债权人请求股东出资的诉权在公司，允许多层穿透会导致公司的独立人格与股东出资法律关系失序。然而，在司法实践中，各地法院对此问题的观点并不统一。
在执行阶段是否能够逐一追加被执行人一人有限公司的股东、股东的股东
最高院立场：法律依据不足，穿透层级受限
(2023)最高法执监25号案中，
泰安中院在执行甲公司与丁公司、泰乙公司等建设工程合同纠纷一案中，申请执行人甲公司申请追加乙公司的股东马某、丙公司为本案被执行人。

最高人民法院院认为，本案的争议焦点为执行程序中能否追加股东的股东为被执行人。《最高人民法院关于执行变更、追加当事人若干问题的规定》规定了依法追加被执行人的情形，但相关主体被追加为被执行人后，能否再以上述主体为被执行人继续追加其股东为被执行人并未明确规定。执行程序中追加被执行人应当以法律明确规定的情形为限，追加股东的股东为被执行人缺乏明确的法律依据。而且，确定实体权利应由审判程序完成，在执行程序中追加被执行人属于不经审判确定了被追加执行人的实体义务，本身已经扩张了生效裁判的效力，适度的扩张可以提升效率，但不能过度扩张。在执行程序中追加被执行人应仅限于一次追加，不能连续追加股东的股东为被执行人。
其他法院观点：可以追加
鹰潭合伙企业中财公司、中奥公司借款合同纠纷一案中，北京市昌平区人民法院对是否追加股东态度反复
①追加一人有限公司的股东——不支持

blocks.content: (2019)京0114执异86号执行裁定以鹰潭合伙企业的追加申请缺乏事实依据为由不予支持申请执行人鹰潭合伙企业以被执行人中奥公司为一人有限责任公司为由，申请追加其股东中晟公司为本案的被执行人。

blocks.text: ②追加一人有限公司的股东——支持

blocks.content: 在(2019)京0114民初10881号判决中根据《最高人民法院关于民事执行中变更、追加当事人若干问题的规定》第二十条规定，作为被执行人的一人有限责任公司，财产不足以清偿生效法律文书确定的债务，股东不能证明公司财产独立于自己的财产，申请执行人申请变更、追加该股东为被执行人，对公司债务承担连带责任的，人民法院应予支持。本案中，中奥公司系只有一个法人股东的有限责任公司，中晟公司系其独资法人股东。已经生效的判决书判决中奥公司对（2012）昌民初字第11459号民事判决书中确定的债务承担连带清偿责任，中晟公司应该就中奥公司的该项债务承担连带责任。法院支持了鹰潭合伙企业申请追加中晟公司为被执行人的诉讼请求。

blocks.text: ③追加一人有限公司股东的股东——不支持

blocks.content: (2020)京0114执异464号执行裁定中，鹰潭合伙企业向法院院申请追加安华实业公司（中晟华夏公司的股东）为被执行人。

blocks.text: 法院认为，执行程序中对追加当事人有明确的法律规定，必须遵循事由法定的原则。本案中，中晟华夏公司作为（2012）昌民初字第11459号民事判决书的被告中奥启天公司的股东，经点鹰潭合伙企业申请，通过诉讼被判决追加为借款合同纠纷案件的被执行人，即中晟华夏公司的被执行人地位系通过追加程序而取得。现鹰潭合伙企业向本院申请追加中晟华夏公司的股东安华实业公司为该案被执行人，其本质应属追加（2012）昌民初字第11459号民事判决书的被告中奥启天公司的股东中晟华夏公司的股东为被执行人，而追加被告的股东的股东作为被执行人，没有法律依据，故对于鹰潭合伙企业的追加请求，法院不予支持。
④追加一人有限公司的股东的股东（一人有限公司的股东也是一人有限公司）——支持

blocks.content: (2021)京0114民初9426号原告鹰潭合伙企业诉被告安华公司、中晟华厦公司、中奥启天公司执行异议之诉一中，法院认为，《最高人民法院关于民事执行中变更、追加当事人若干问题的规定》第二十条规定“作为被执行人的一人有限责任公司，财产不足以清偿生效法律文书确定的债务，股东不能证明公司财产独立于自己的财产，申请执行人申请变更、追加该股东为被执行人，对公司债务承担连带责任的，人民法院应予支持”。本案中，中晟华夏公司系只有一个法人股东的有限责任公司，安华公司系其唯一股东。已经生效的判决书判决中晟华夏公司对（2012）昌民初字第11459号民事判决书中确定的债务承担连带清偿责任，安华公司应该就中晟华夏公司的该项债务承担连带责任。现点鹰潭合伙企业申请追加安华公司为被执行人，本院予以支持。

(2022)粤01民终8731号案中二审法院认为，原告与唐鋆公司发生案涉债权债务关系时，唐鋆公司为本真行公司法人独资的一人有限责任公司，本真行公司是自然人独资企业，其唯一股东为羊远春。本真行公司提供的审计报告不足以证明本真行公司、唐鋆公司的财产相互独立，故原告申请追加本真行公司为被执行人符合法律规定。由于本真行公司作为被执行人亦无其他可供执行的财产，羊远春在本案中则自认其无证据证实本真行公司财产独立于其个人财产，故原告申请同时追加羊远春为被执行人，符合《最高人民法院关于民事执行中变更、追加当事人若干问题》第二十条的规定，法院予以支持。

blocks.text: 诉讼阶段是否能够要求一人有限公司的股东、股东的股东一同承担连带责任

blocks.title: 观点一：应当承担连带责任

blocks.content: (2020)粤01民终12207号案中，大唐公司向一审法院起诉请求：判令苏商公司向大唐公司支付欠付货款及利息；判令太平洋建设公司、太平洋集团公司对苏商公司上述诉讼请求承担连带责任；一审法院认为，苏商公司为一人有限责任公司，股东太平洋建设公司不能证明公司财产独立于股东自己的财产，应当对公司债务承担连带责任。太平洋建设公司亦为一人有限责任公司，股东太平洋建设公司不能证明公司财产独立于股东自己的财产，应当对公司债务承担连带责任。大唐公司要求太平洋建设公司、太平洋集团公司对苏商公司的上述债务承担连带清偿责任，符合法律规定，一审法院予以支持。二审法院维持原判。

(2022)鲁1083民初96号案中，原告提出诉讼请求：要求威海顺丰乳山营业部赔偿货物损失、保证金损失；要求威海顺丰顺意达分公司、威海顺丰公司、山东顺丰公司、顺丰速运有限公司对上述债务承担连带清偿责任。法院认为，“一人有限责任公司的股东不能证明公司财产独立于股东自己的财产的，应当对公司债务承担连带责任。”本案中，因威海顺丰乳山营业部、威海顺丰顺意达分公司系威海顺丰公司设立的分公司，不具有法人资格，其民事责任由威海顺丰公司承担；山东顺丰公司系威海顺丰公司的一人股东、顺丰速运有限公司系山东顺丰公司的一人股东，其均不能提供证明公司财产独立于股东自己的财产，应当对威海顺丰公司的债务承担连带责任。
另外，广东法院做出的(2019)粤0113民初12036号、(2021)粤2071民初36246号判决均支持了原告要求一人有限责任公司的股东和股东的股东承担连带责任的诉讼请求。

blocks.title: 观点二：承担连带责任于法无据

blocks.content: (2021)苏0981民初6655号案中，原告金螳螂公司与被告恒冠公司、恒大南京公司、恒大公司装饰装修合同纠纷一案，法院认为，关于恒大南京公司、恒大公司是否承担责任的问题。《中华人民共和国公司法》第六十二条规定，一人有限责任公司应当在每一会计年度终了时编制财务会计报告，并经会计师事务所审计。第六十三条规定，一人有限责任公司的股东不能证明公司财产独立于股东自己的财产的，应当对公司债务承担连带责任。本案中，因恒冠公司系一人有限公司，其股东为恒大南京公司，恒大南京公司未能提供充分证据证明公司财产独立于其财产的，应当对恒冠公司的上述债务承担连带责任。关于金螳螂公司要求恒大公司承担连带责任的请求。根据《中华人民共和国民法典》第一百七十八条第三款规定，连带责任由法律规定或者当事人约定，对于一人有限公司的独资股东的独资股东应否承担连带责任并无法律明确约定，美瑞德公司亦未举证证明双方对此有约定，故本院依法不予支持。
(2020)浙0127民初309号案中，原告钜元公司诉被告尚雅公司、华普公司、艾雯公司建设工程施工合同纠纷一案，法院认为，被告尚雅公司系由被告华普公司100%控股的一人公司，无证据证明尚雅公司财产独立于华普公司，故被告华普公司应与尚雅公司承担连带清偿责任。被告艾雯公司是被告华普公司一人股东，但并非尚雅公司的股东，对被告要求艾雯公司承担连带清偿责任的诉讼请求，法院不予支持。二审法院维持原判。

blocks.text: 争议焦点与实务建议
从上述司法实践可以看出，一人有限公司股东的股东是否应当承担连带责任的争议焦点主要集中在以下几个方面
1.“生效法律文书确定的债务”的解读：部分法院认为《最高人民法院关于民事执行中变更、追加当事人若干问题的规定》第二十条中的“生效法律文书确定的债务”应理解为经生效法律文书确认的基础债权债务，不能随意扩大解释，否则会导致执行程序中过度扩张生效裁判的效力。
2.是否违背公司独立人格与股东出资法律关系：反对多层穿透追加股东责任的观点强调，公司的独立人格和股东的有限责任是公司法的基本原则，若轻易允许连环追加，会使公司的独立人格与股东出资法律关系陷入混乱，影响市场交易的稳定性和可预期性。
3.举证责任的分配与财产混同的认定难度：要求股东证明公司财产独立于自己的财产，这对于股东来说可能存在一定的举证难度。而在多层一人公司的情况下，各层级股东都需要承担相应的举证责任，否则将面临承担连带责任的风险，这也在一定程度上增加了司法实践中的认定复杂性。
实务建议
对于债权人：如果遇到债务人是多层架构的一人公司，且各层级股东无法证明公司财产与自己财产独立的情况，可以尝试在诉讼阶段一次性将各层级股东列为被告，要求其承担连带责任，同时积极收集相关证据，如公司的财务报表、资金往来记录等，以证明公司与股东之间存在财产混同的迹象。
对于投资人：在设立一人公司或多层级控股架构时，要严格规范公司的财务管理和资金运作，确保公司财产与个人或其他股东财产相互独立，保留好完整的财务凭证和审计报告等证据，以避免在出现债务纠纷时被债权人追究连带责任。

references: 《最高人民法院关于民事执行中变更、追加当事人若干问题的规定》（2020修正）
zh.conclusion: 一人有限公司股东的股东是否应当承担连带责任，在不同地区、不同法院之间存在着较大的分歧和不确定性。这一问题的复杂性要求我们在面对相关法律事务时，必须保持审慎的态度，综合考虑各种因素，以维护自身或当事人的合法权益，同时也期待未来相关法律规定的进一步明确和完善，为司法实践提供更清晰明确的指引。

en.title: Should the shareholders of a one-person limited company bear joint and several liabilities?Different viewpoints and judgment ideas in judicial practice
en.lead: In the field of company law, whether the shareholders of a one-person limited company (the shareholders of a one-person limited company are also one-person limited companies) should bear joint and several liability for the debts of the company has always been a matter of great concern and controversy.This issue is not only related to the realization of the interests of creditors, but also affects the definition of the responsibilities of shareholders of companies and the stability of market trading order.This article will explore this issue in depth in light of relevant laws and regulations and judicial practice cases.

blocks.text: Legal provisions lay the foundation for liability

Article 23 of the Company Law of the People's Republic of China clearly stipulates that if the shareholders of a one-person company cannot prove the independence of the company's property, they shall be jointly and severally liable for the debts of the company, which provides a legal basis for investigating the liability of the shareholders of a one-person limited company.However, it does not mention whether "shareholders of shareholders" can be held accountable.

Article 20 of the Provisions of the Supreme People's Court on Several Issues Concerning the Alteration and Addition of Parties in Civil Execution (2020 Amendment) further clarifies that a one-person limited liability company as the person subjected to execution has insufficient property to pay off the debts specified in the effective legal documents, and the shareholders cannot prove that the company's property is independent of their own property. If the applicant for execution applies for the alteration or addition of the shareholder as the person subjected to execution and assumes joint and several liability for the debts of the company, the people's court shall support it.These provisions constitute the basic legal framework for the joint and several liability of shareholders of one-person limited companies.
Significant differences in judicial views

The seminar on the" Application of the Connection between the Old and the New Company Law "jointly organized by the First Intermediate People's Court of Shanghai and the Shanghai Law Society in consultation with the Law Research Association of the Shanghai Municipal Law Society pointed out that if the problem is generalized to" whether the creditor can investigate the liability of the shareholder for capital contribution ", the answer is no.The reason is that the capital contribution subscribed by the shareholders constitutes the liability property of the company, and the creditor's right of action to request the shareholder's capital contribution lies in the company. Allowing multi-level penetration will lead to the disorder of the legal relationship between the independent personality of the company and the shareholder's capital contribution.However, in judicial practice, the courts of all localities have different views on this issue.
Can the shareholders of the one-person limited company subject to execution and the shareholders of the shareholders be added one by one at the stage of execution
Supreme Court position: insufficient legal basis, limited penetration level
(2023) Case No. 25 of the Supreme People's Court,

In the case of a dispute over the execution of a construction contract between Company A and Company D, Company B and other construction projects, the applicant Company A applies to add the shareholders of Company B, Ma and C, as the persons to be executed in the case.

The Court of the Supreme People's Court holds that the focus of the dispute in this case is whether the shareholder of the additional shareholder in the execution procedure can be the executed person.The Provisions of the Supreme People's Court on Several Issues Concerning the Alteration and Addition of Parties to Enforcement stipulate the circumstances of adding the person subjected to execution according to law, but after the relevant subject is added as the person subjected to execution, whether the above-mentioned subject can continue to add its shareholders as the person subjected to execution is not clearly stipulated.The addition of the person subjected to execution in the execution procedure shall be limited to the circumstances clearly stipulated by law, and the shareholder of the additional shareholder as the person subjected to execution lacks a clear legal basis.Moreover, the determination of substantive rights should be completed by trial procedures, and the addition of the person subjected to execution in the execution procedure belongs to the substantive obligation of the person subjected to additional execution determined without trial, which has expanded the effectiveness of the effective judgment itself. Appropriate expansion can improve efficiency, but it cannot be excessive expansion.The addition of the person subjected to execution in the execution procedure shall be limited to one additional addition, and the shareholder who cannot continuously add shareholders shall be the person subjected to execution.
Opinions of other courts: may be added

In the case of a dispute over a loan contract between the China Finance Company and the China Olympic Company of the Yingtan Partnership Enterprise, the People's Court of Changping District, Beijing Municipality has repeated its attitude on whether to add shareholders
① Additional shareholders of one-person limited company - not supported

blocks.content: (2019) Jing 0114 Zhiyi No.86 Execution Ruling does not support the applicant for execution on the grounds that the additional application of Yingtan Partnership lacks factual basis. Yingtan Partnership applies to add its shareholder Zhongsheng Company as the executed person of the case on the grounds that the executed person Zhong'ao Company is a one-person limited liability company.

blocks.text: ② Additional shareholders of one-person limited company – support

blocks.content: In accordance with the provisions of Article 20 of the Provisions of the Supreme People's Court on Several Issues Concerning the Alteration and Addition of Parties in Civil Execution in the Judgment No. 10881 [2019] Jing 0114 of the Early Republic of China, a one-person limited liability company as the person subjected to execution has insufficient property to pay off the debts determined by the effective legal documents, and the shareholders are unable to prove that the company's property is independent of their own property. If the applicant for execution applies to change or add the shareholder as the person subjected to execution and bears joint and several liabilities for the debts of the company, the people's court shall support it.In this case, Sino-Ao Company is a limited liability company with only one juridical person shareholder, and Sino-sheng Company is its sole juridical person shareholder.The effective judgment determines that the Sino-Ao Company shall be jointly and severally liable for the debts determined in the Civil Judgment No. 11459 [2012] of the Changmin Chu Zi, and Sino-Ao Company shall be jointly and severally liable for the debts of the Sino-Ao Company.The court supported the claim of Yingtan Partnership for adding Zhongsheng Company as the person subjected to execution.

blocks.text: ③ Additional shareholders of one-person limited company - not supported

blocks.content: (2020) In the execution ruling No. 464 of Jing 0114, Yingtan Partnership applied to the court for adding Anhua Industrial Company (a shareholder of Zhongsheng Huaxia Company) as the person subjected to execution.

blocks.text: The court considers that there are clear legal provisions on the additional parties in the execution procedure, and the principle of legality must be followed.In this case, Zhongsheng Huaxia Company, as a shareholder of Zhong'ao Qitian Company, the defendant in the Civil Judgment No. 11459 [2012] of Changmin Chu Zi, upon the application of Dianyingtan Partnership Enterprise, was adjudged to be the executed person in the loan contract dispute case through litigation, that is, the status of the executed person of Zhongsheng Huaxia Company was obtained through the additional procedure.Now, Yingtan Partnership Enterprise applies to this Court for adding Anhua Industrial Company, the shareholder of Zhongsheng Huaxia Company, as the person subjected to execution in the case, which in essence should be the defendant in the Civil Judgment No. 11459 [2012] of Changmin Chu Zi, the shareholder of Zhongsheng Huaxia Company, the shareholder of Zhong'ao Qitian Company, as the person subjected to execution, and the shareholder of the shareholder of the additional defendant as the person subjected to execution, without legal basis, so the court shall not support the additional request of Yingtan Partnership Enterprise.
④ Shareholders of additional shareholders of one-person limited companies (shareholders of one-person limited companies are also one-person limited companies) - support

blocks.content: (2021) Jing 0114 [Early Republic of China] No.9426, plaintiff Yingtan Partnership v. defendant Anhua Company, Zhongsheng Huaxia Company and Zhong'ao Qitian Company, in the first lawsuit of objection to execution, the court considers that Article 20 of the Provisions of the Supreme People's Court on Several Issues Concerning the Alteration and Addition of Parties in Civil Execution stipulates that" If the property of a one-person limited liability company as the person subjected to execution is insufficient to pay off the debts determined by the effective legal documents, and the shareholder is unable to prove that the company's property is independent of his own property, the applicant for execution applies for changing or adding the shareholder as the person subjected to execution, and assumes joint and several liability for the debts of the company, the people's court shall support it ".In this case, Zhongsheng Huaxia Company is a limited liability company with only one juridical person shareholder, and Anhua Company is its only shareholder.The effective judgment decides that Zhongsheng Huaxia Company shall be jointly and severally liable for the debts determined in the civil judgment No. 11459 [2012] of the Changmin Chu Zi, and Anhua Company shall be jointly and severally liable for the debts of Zhongsheng Huaxia Company.The Court hereby supports the application of Yingtan Partnership Enterprise for adding Anhua Company as the executed person.

(2022) The court of second instance in the case of Yue 01 Min Zhong 8731 holds that when the plaintiff and Tang Yun Company had a relationship involving creditor's rights and debts, Tang Yun Company was a one-person limited liability company wholly owned by a juridical person of Benzhenhang Company, which was a sole proprietorship enterprise of natural persons, and its sole shareholder was Yang Yuanchun.The audit report provided by Benzhenhang Company is insufficient to prove that the property of Benzhenhang Company and Tang Yun Company is independent of each other, so the plaintiff's application for adding Benzhenhang Company as the executed person conforms to the law.As Benzhenhang Company, as the person subjected to execution, has no other property available for execution, and Yang Yuanchun, in this case, admits that he has no evidence to prove that the property of Benzhenhang Company is independent of his personal property, so the plaintiff applies to add Yang Yuanchun as the person subjected to execution at the same time, which conforms to the provisions of Article 20 of the Supreme People's Court on Several Issues Concerning the Alteration and Addition of Parties in Civil Execution, and the court supports it.

blocks.text: Can the shareholders of a one-person limited company and the shareholders of the shareholders be required to bear joint and several liabilities at the litigation stage

blocks.title: Viewpoint 1: Joint and several liability shall be borne

blocks.content: (2020) Guangdong 01 Civil Division 12207 Case, Datang Company brought a lawsuit to the court of first instance to request: to order Suzhou Commerce Company to pay the arrears and interest to Datang Company;Order the Pacific Construction Company and the Pacific Group Company to bear joint and several liability for the above-mentioned claims of the Sushang Company;The court of first instance held that the Sushang Company is a one-person limited liability company, and the shareholder Pacific Construction Company cannot prove that the company's property is independent of the shareholder's own property, and shall be jointly and severally liable for the company's debts.Pacific Construction Company is also a one-person limited liability company. The shareholder Pacific Construction Company cannot prove that the company's property is independent of the shareholder's own property, and shall be jointly and severally liable for the company's debts.Datang requires the Pacific Construction Company and the Pacific Group Company to bear joint and several liability for the above-mentioned debts of the Sushang Company, which conforms to the provisions of the law and is supported by the court of first instance.The court of second instance upheld the original judgment.

(2022) Case No. 96 of the early Republic of China of Lu 1083, the plaintiff filed a claim: to request Weihai Shunfeng Rushan Business Department to compensate for the loss of goods and the loss of deposit;Require Weihai Shunfeng Shunyida Branch, Weihai Shunfeng Company, Shandong Shunfeng Company and Shunfeng Express Co., Ltd. to bear joint and several liability for the above debts.The court held that "if the shareholders of a one-person limited liability company are unable to prove that the company's property is independent of the shareholders' own property, they shall be jointly and severally liable for the debts of the company." In this case, because the Weihai Shunfeng Rushan Business Department and the Weihai Shunfeng Shunyida Branch are branches established by the Weihai Shunfeng Company, they do not have the status of juridical persons, and their civil liabilities shall be borne by the Weihai Shunfeng Company;Shandong Shunfeng Company is a one-person shareholder of Weihai Shunfeng Company, and Shunfeng Express Co., Ltd. is a one-person shareholder of Shandong Shunfeng Company. None of them can provide proof that the company's property is independent of the shareholder's own property, and they shall be jointly and severally liable for the debts of Weihai Shunfeng Company.

In addition, the judgments No. 12036 (2019) Yue 0113 and No. 36246 (2021) Yue 2071 made by the Guangdong Court supported the plaintiff's claim that the shareholders of a one-person limited liability company and the shareholders of the shareholders shall bear joint and several liability.

blocks.title: Viewpoint 2: There is no legal basis for joint and several liability

blocks.content: In the case of (2021) Su 0981 Min Chu No. 6655, in the dispute over decoration and construction contract between plaintiff Gold Mantis Company and defendants Hengguan Company, Evergrande Nanjing Company, and Evergrande Company, the court held that regarding whether Evergrande Nanjing Company and Evergrande Company should bear liability. Article 62 of the Company Law of the People's Republic of China stipulates that a one-person limited liability company shall prepare financial and accounting reports at the end of each fiscal year and shall be audited by an accounting firm. Article 63 stipulates that if a shareholder of a one-person limited liability company is unable to prove that the company's property is independent of the shareholder's own property, he shall be jointly and severally liable for the debts of the company. In this case, since Hengguan Company is a one-person limited company, its shareholder is Evergrande Nanjing Company, and Evergrande Nanjing Company fails to provide sufficient evidence to prove that the company's property is independent of its property, it shall be jointly and severally liable for the above-mentioned debts of Hengguan Company. Regarding Gold Mantis Company's request for Evergrande Company to bear joint and several liability. According to the provisions of paragraph 3 of Article 178 of the Civil Code of the People's Republic of China, joint and several liability shall be stipulated by law or agreed upon by the parties. There is no clear agreement in law on whether the sole proprietorship shareholders of a one-person limited company shall bear joint and several liability, nor has Merid provided evidence to prove that both parties have agreed on this, so the Court does not support it according to law.

(2020) In the case No. 309 [2020] of the early Republic of China, the plaintiff Juyuan Company v. the defendant Shangya Company, Huapu Company and Aiwen Company over a dispute over a construction contract for a construction project, the court held that the defendant Shangya Company is a one-person company 100% controlled by the defendant Huapu Company, and there is no evidence to prove that the property of Shangya Company is independent of Huapu Company, so the defendant Huapu Company shall bear joint and several liability for repayment with Shangya Company.Defendant Ai Wen Company is a shareholder of Defendant Huapu Company, but not a shareholder of Shangya Company. The court shall not support the defendant's claim that Ai Wen Company should bear joint and several liability for repayment.The court of second instance upheld the original judgment.

blocks.text: Focus of Disputes and Practical Suggestions

From the above judicial practice, it can be seen that the focus of the dispute over whether the shareholders of a one-person limited company should bear joint and several liability mainly focuses on the following aspects

1. Interpretation of" Debt Determined by Effective Legal Documents ": Some courts believe that the" debt determined by effective legal documents "in Article 20 of the Provisions of the Supreme People's Court on Several Issues Concerning the Alteration and Addition of Parties in Civil Execution shall be understood as the basic creditor's rights and debts confirmed by effective legal documents, and the interpretation shall not be expanded at will, otherwise the effect of effective judgments will be excessively expanded in the execution procedure.

2. Whether it violates the legal relationship between the independent personality of the company and the capital contribution of shareholders: The view against multi-level penetration and additional shareholder liability emphasizes that the independent personality of the company and the limited liability of shareholders are the basic principles of the Company Law. If serial additions are allowed easily, the legal relationship between the independent personality of the company and the capital contribution of shareholders will be thrown into confusion, affecting the stability and predictability of market transactions.

3. Difficulty in determining the distribution of the burden of proof and the confusion of property: shareholders are required to prove that the company's property is independent of their own property, which may be difficult for shareholders to prove.In the case of multi-layered one-person companies, shareholders at all levels need to bear the corresponding burden of proof, otherwise they will face the risk of joint and several liability, which also increases the complexity of identification in judicial practice to a certain extent.
Practical suggestions

For creditors: If the debtor is a single-person company with a multi-layered structure, and the shareholders at all levels are unable to prove that the company's property is independent of their own property, they may try to list the shareholders at all levels as defendants at one time in the litigation stage, requiring them to bear joint and several liability, and actively collect relevant evidence, such as the company's financial statements and capital transaction records, to prove that there are signs of property confusion between the company and shareholders.

For investors: When establishing a one-person company or a multi-level holding structure, the financial management and capital operation of the company shall be strictly standardized, so as to ensure that the company's property is independent of the property of individuals or other shareholders, and complete financial vouchers, audit reports and other evidence shall be retained to avoid being investigated for joint and several liability by creditors in case of debt disputes.

en.conclusion: Whether the shareholders of a one-person limited company shall bear joint and several liability or not, there are great differences and uncertainties among different regions and courts.The complexity of this issue requires us to maintain a prudent attitude and comprehensively consider various factors when facing relevant legal affairs in order to safeguard the legitimate rights and interests of ourselves or the parties concerned, and at the same time, we look forward to the further clarification and improvement of relevant legal provisions in the future to provide clearer and clearer guidance for judicial practice.

zh.title: 新《公司法》“横向穿透”：守护债权人权益的有力武器
zh.lead: 新《公司法》第23条首次明确“横向人格否认”规则（即横向穿透），规定股东控制的多家公司若滥用法人独立地位逃避债务，需对任一公司债务承担连带责任。这一规则填补了旧法空白，对债权人保护和企业合规提出新要求。本文结合立法演变、核心要件、典型案例及实务难点，为企业与债权人提供操作指引。

blocks.text: 一、横向穿透规则的核心要义
新《公司法》第二十三条明确规定，股东利用其控制的两个以上公司实施滥用公司法人独立地位和股东有限责任行为，逃避债务，严重损害公司债权人利益的，各公司应当对任一公司的债务承担连带责任。这意味着，当股东通过操控多个公司来逃避债务时，债权人有权要求这些关联公司共同承担债务责任，突破了以往公司独立人格和有限责任的限制，实现了对债权人的有力保护。
横向穿透规则实质上蕴含着纵向法人人格否认和反向法人人格否认的两种情形。通过揭开公司面棉纱使其背后的股东承担连带责任，然后揭开股东的面纱，使股东控制的其他公司对股东的债权人承担连带责任。虽然公司法未明确规定，但基于“三角刺破”规则的逻辑，控制股东的债权人在特定情况下，可要求控制股东及其控制的多家公司作为一个责任整体承担连带责任。
二、适用横向穿透规则的关键要件
（一）主体要件
原告通常为公司债权人，而被告则是同一股东控制的两个以上公司。这里的控制主体不仅包括显名股东，还涵盖隐名股东和实际控制人。从目的解释来看，横向人格否认制度旨在防止公司人格独立和有限责任被滥用，将隐名股东和实际控制人纳入控制主体范围，更有利于保护债权人利益，且不会对其他股东造成不可预见的不利影响。
（二）行为要件
控制主体需利用其控制的公司实施滥用行为，主要包括人格混同、过度支配与控制两类情形。在横向人格否认中，若控制行为超出合理范围，构成过度支配与控制，各横向关联公司应承担连带责任；若控制行为在合理范围内，则需考察是否形成人格混同，若形成则承担连带责任。
1.人格混同

认定公司人格与股东人格是否存在混同，最根本的判断标准是公司是否具有独立意志和独立利益，最主要的表现是公司的财务或者财产与股东的财务或者财产是否混同，公司的财产是否独立。出现以下情形之一的，可以认定为财务或者财产混同：（1）股东随意无偿调拨公司资金或者财产，不作财务记载的；（2）股东用公司的资金偿还股东个人的债务，或者调拨资金到关联公司，不作财务记载的；（3）公司账簿与股东账簿不分；（4）股东自身收益与公司盈利不加区分，致使双方利益不清；（5）公司的财产记载于股东名下，由股东占有、使用。
在出现财务或者财产混同的情况下，往往同时出现以下混同：公司业务和股东业务混同；公司员工与股东员工混同，特别是财务人员混同；公司住所与股东住所混同。人民法院在审理这类案件时，关键要看是否构成财务或者财产混同，而不要求同时具备其他方面的混同，其他方面的混同往往只是财务混同或者财产混同的补强。

(2020)最高法民终1084号案中，最高院认为，银燕公司、百富勤公司之间具有关联关系。关联公司的人员、业务、财产等方面交叉或混同，导致各自财产无法区分，丧失独立人格的，构成人格混同。关联公司人格混同，严重损害债权人利益的，关联公司相互之间应对外部债务承担连带责任。百富勤公司在一审中认可银燕公司代其收取部分股权转让款的事实，并在回答一审法院询问“财务如何处理”时称“不清楚”，银燕公司也未提交证据证明其代百富勤公司收取相关股权转让款具有合同依据。且银燕公司、百富勤公司对于代收的部分款项，是否已经返还给百富勤公司等事实均未提供证据予以证实。因百富勤公司、银燕公司之间存在没有合同依据的利益输送，导致两家公司财产边界不清、财务混同，一审法院判决银燕公司应对百富勤公司债务承担连带责任并无不当。

(2021)最高法民再342号案中，二审法院认为金元贸易公司与汇业贸易公司、永兴仪器厂表面上彼此独立，但三公司（或厂）均受孙和平或其直系亲属控制，孙和平或其直系亲属作为控股股东或实际控制人控制三公司（或厂），滥用控制权使三公司（或厂）财产边界不清，利益相互输送，丧失人格独立性，成为逃避债务的工具，严重损害金元贸易公司债权人的利益。由此，一审判决以金元贸易公司、原金元贸易公司、汇业贸易公司等存在人格、财产混同为由，未支持汇业贸易公司、永兴仪器厂对案涉集体土地使用权及临时建筑的拆迁补偿部分排除执行的诉求，有相应的事实和法律依据，二审判决予以改判有误，最高人民法院予以纠正。
2.过度支配与控制

公司一旦被某一股东滥用控制权，就不再具有独立意志和独立利益，其独立人格就会沦为道具，如仍然恪守公司独立人格，就会严重损害公司债权人利益，此时应当否认公司人格。在多个关联公司由同一人、夫妻、母子或者家族控制的场合，在认定是否应当否定公司人格时，重点就要考察是否存在滥用控制行为的情形。以下情形，一般可以认定为滥用控制行为：同一股东控制的子公司向其他子公司输送利益；关联公司与其他公司进行交易，收益归关联公司一方，损失却由关联公司另一方承担；先抽逃公司资金或解散公司，再以原设备、场所、人员及相同经营目的另设公司，从而逃避原公司债务。

blocks.content: (2019)苏民终1528号案中，关于国发节能公司、国发后勤公司、郭留成与国发华企公司是否人格混同，是否应对国发华企公司对国电光伏公司的案涉债务承担连带清偿责任的问题，法院认为法院经审理，对国发节能公司、国发后勤公司、郭留成与国发华企公司之间的关系及责任承担作出明确认定，具体结果如下：

blocks.text: ①认定国发节能公司、国发后勤公司与国发华企公司人格混同，需承担连带清偿责任

国发节能公司、国发后勤公司、国发华企公司在住所地、联系方式、高管人员、业务范围等方面存在混同或交叉。国电光伏公司提供的工商信息、招聘信息、年度报告显示，三公司住所地均位于北京市西城区 ，联系电话相同，国发后勤公司与国发华企公司的电子邮箱一致。同时，郭留成、韩霞、王学光等在三家公司交叉任职，且三公司经营范围均涉及机械、电器等相关业务。相关主体虽提出异议，但因未能提供有效证据证明，法院对其主张不予采信。

从一审法院调取的国发华企公司五个银行账户往来明细来看，能证明三公司存在财务混同。2011-2018年期间，三公司之间存在大量资金往来，多数资金用途标注不明；国发华企公司的项目回款和财政补贴频繁、大额转出给国发节能公司、国发后勤公司；国发节能公司、国发后勤公司向国发华企公司转入的资金与国发华企公司的日常经营支出基本持平。国发节能公司、国发后勤公司、郭留成拒绝提供财务账册配合司法审计，也未提供其他反驳证据，一审法院采信国电光伏公司对银行流水的核查意见，认定三公司财务混同并无不当。二审中，相关主体仅以银行流水不能完整反映资金流向为由否定财务混同，法院不予认可。

②认定郭留成作为实际控制人过度支配与控制公司，需承担连带清偿责任：根据一审查明事实，郭留成通过股权控制链条，即持有国发后勤公司97.83%的股权，国发后勤公司持有国发节能公司99.69%的股权，国发节能公司持有国发华企公司93.75%的股权，能够实际控制、支配国发节能公司、国发华企公司，且其同时为两公司个人股东并担任公司高管人员。依据《最高人民法院关于适用〈中华人民共和国民事诉讼法〉的解释》第一百零八条第一款规定，郭留成系三公司实际控制人具有高度可能性。国发节能公司、国发后勤公司、郭留成上诉否认但未提供反驳证据，应承担不利后果。郭留成作为实际控制人，对公司过度支配与控制，致使三公司财产边界不清、财务混同，丧失人格独立性，导致国发华企公司无法清偿欠付国电光伏公司的大额债务，严重损害了公司债权人利益，因此应对公司债务承担连带责任。
因此，法院认为国发节能公司、国发后勤公司、郭留成的上诉请求缺乏事实和法律依据，不予支持。一审判决认定事实清楚，适用法律正确，予以维持，即驳回上诉，维持原判。国发节能环保发展集团有限公司向最高人民法院提出的再审申请也被驳回。
（三）主观要件
股东存在逃避债务的故意。这一要件通常结合控制行为和滥用行为进行判断，实践中，在满足其他要件的情况下，一般可完成对该要件的证明，无需债权人另行举证。
（四）结果要件
必须严重损害公司债权人利益。判断标准通常为债务人不能清偿到期债务。若债权人能证明存在滥用行为，一般可推定满足结果要件，无需证明债务人无偿债能力。而且，结果要件不以其他方式无法救济为前提，债权人有权选择直接适用横向穿透规则来维护自身权益。
三、债权人维权的关键要点
（一）证明责任

在适用横向穿透规则时，债权人承担证明责任，需证明滥用行为、逃避债务以及严重损害债权人利益等要件事实成立。然而，由于滥用行为具有隐蔽性，实践中债权人举证存在困难。对此，债权人可采用“初步证据标准+申请法院调取证据”的方式降低证明难度。例如，债权人提供关联公司发布的招聘信息、营业执照复印件等初步证据，证明公司之间存在人员、业务混同的可能性，法院可根据公平原则和诚实信用原则，要求公司或股东进一步证明不存在滥用行为，否则将认定待证事实成立。
（二）诉讼程序
1.诉讼地位
债权人应根据债务是否已由生效法律文书确认，来确定被告和第三人。若债务已确认，债权人另行提起公司人格否认诉讼时，列股东为被告，公司为第三人；若债务未确认，债权人就其与公司之间的债务提起诉讼的同时，一并提起公司人格否认诉讼，要求股东承担责任的，列公司和股东为共同被告；
债权人与公司之间的债务尚未经生效法律文书确认，债权人直接提起公司人格否认诉讼，要求股东对债务承担责任的，人民法院应当向债权人释明，追加公司为共同被告。债权人拒绝追加的，裁定驳回起诉。
2.管辖法院
若债权人同时起诉债务人公司和相关主体，根据基础债权债务关系的性质确定管辖法院；若债权人在取得对债务人公司的债权确认生效判决后另行起诉，则由侵权行为地或者被告住所地人民法院管辖，债务人公司住所地法院通常具有管辖权。
3.诉讼时效
若同时起诉，按基础债权债务关系确定诉讼时效；若另行起诉，则适用一般侵权关于诉讼时效的规定，从债权人知道或应当知道权利受损及义务人之日起计算三年。
（三）执行阶段

根据《最高人民法院关于民事执行中变更、追加当事人若干问题的规定》，追加当事人应严格遵循法定原则，即变更、追加执行当事人应当严格限定于法律、司法解释明确规定的情形。主体混同、人格混同、财产混同不属于法律、司法解释规定的追加被执行人的法定情形。法人人格否认不能在执行阶段直接追加相关主体责任，公司人格否认诉讼是追究股东或关联公司责任的必经程序。只有符合法定条件，如企业发生分立、合并等情况，执行法院才能依法变更、追加被执行人。

references: 最高人民法院关于适用《中华人民共和国公司法》若干问题的规定（二）（2020修正）
《关于适用<中华人民共和国民事诉讼法>的解释（2022修正）》
《最高人民法院关于民事执行中变更、追加当事人若干问题的规定》
《全国法院民商事审判工作会议纪要》
最高人民法院指导案例第15号
最高人民法院指导案例第68号
zh.conclusion: 新《公司法》的横向穿透规则为债权人提供了强大的法律武器。在复杂的商业环境中，债权人应充分了解并运用这一规则，积极收集证据，通过合法的诉讼程序维护自身权益。同时，公司及其股东也应严格遵守法律法规，保持公司人格独立，避免陷入不必要的法律风险。若您在相关方面遇到法律问题，欢迎随时联系我们的律所，我们将竭诚为您提供专业的法律服务。

en.title: The new Company Law" horizontal penetration ": a powerful weapon to protect the rights and interests of creditors
en.lead: Article 23 of the new Company Law specifies for the first time the rule of" horizontal personality denial "(that is, horizontal penetration), which stipulates that if a number of companies controlled by shareholders abuse the independent status of juridical persons to evade their debts, they shall be jointly and severally liable for the debts of any company.This rule fills the gap in the old law and puts forward new requirements for creditor protection and enterprise compliance.This article combines legislative evolution, core elements, typical cases and practical difficulties to provide operational guidance for enterprises and creditors.

blocks.text: I. The core meaning of the horizontal penetration rule

Article 23 of the new Company Law clearly stipulates that if a shareholder abuses the independent status of a company as a juridical person and the limited liability of a shareholder by using two or more companies under his control to evade debts and seriously damages the interests of creditors of the company, each company shall be jointly and severally liable for the debts of any company.This means that when shareholders evade their debts by manipulating multiple companies, creditors have the right to require these affiliated companies to jointly assume debt liability, which breaks through the restrictions of independent personality and limited liability of companies in the past and realizes the effective protection of creditors.

The horizontal penetration rule essentially contains two kinds of situations: the vertical denial of juridical personality and the reverse denial of juridical personality.By uncovering the veil of the company, the shareholders behind it shall bear joint and several liability, and then uncovering the veil of shareholders, so that other companies controlled by shareholders shall bear joint and several liability to the creditors of shareholders.Although the Company Law does not specify it, based on the logic of the "triangular puncture" rule, the creditor of the controlling shareholder may require the controlling shareholder and the multiple companies under its control to bear joint and several liability as a whole under specific circumstances.
II. Key elements for the application of the horizontal penetration rule
(1) Main elements

The plaintiff is usually a creditor of the company, while the defendant is two or more companies controlled by the same shareholder.The controlling subjects here include not only the obvious shareholders, but also the anonymous shareholders and the actual controllers.From the perspective of purpose interpretation, the horizontal personality denial system aims to prevent the independence of corporate personality and the abuse of limited liability, and to include anonymous shareholders and actual controllers in the scope of control subjects, which is more conducive to protecting the interests of creditors and will not cause unforeseeable adverse effects on other shareholders.
(2) Elements of conduct

The control subject needs to use the company under its control to commit abuse, which mainly includes two types of circumstances: confusion of personality, excessive domination and control.In the horizontal denial of personality, if the control behavior exceeds the reasonable scope and constitutes excessive domination and control, the horizontal affiliated companies shall bear joint and several liabilities;If the control act is within a reasonable range, it is necessary to examine whether it forms a confusion of personality, and if it does, it shall bear joint and several liability.
1. Personality confusion

To determine whether there is any confusion between the corporate personality and the shareholder personality, the most fundamental criterion is whether the company has independent will and independent interests, and the most important performance is whether the financial or property of the company is mixed with the financial or property of the shareholders, and whether the property of the company is independent.In case of any of the following circumstances, it may be recognized as financial or property confusion: (1) The shareholders allocate the company's funds or property free of charge at will without making financial records;(2) Shareholders use the company's funds to repay the debts of individual shareholders or allocate funds to affiliated companies without making financial records;(3) The company's account books are not separated from the shareholders' account books;(4) The shareholders' own profits are not differentiated from the profits of the company, resulting in unclear interests of both parties;(5) The company's property shall be recorded in the name of the shareholder and shall be possessed and used by the shareholder.

Under the circumstances of financial or property confusion, the following confusion often occurs at the same time: confusion of corporate business and shareholder business;The employees of the company are confused with the employees of shareholders, especially the financial personnel;The domicile of a company is confused with that of a shareholder.When the people's court hears such cases, the key is to see whether it constitutes financial or property confusion, and does not require confusion in other aspects at the same time. The confusion in other aspects is often only the reinforcement of financial or property confusion.

(2020) In the Supreme People's Court Case No. 1084, the Supreme People's Court held that there was an affiliated relationship between Yinyan Company and Bavarian.If the personnel, business and property of an affiliated company are crossed or confused, resulting in the inability to distinguish their respective property and the loss of independent personality, it constitutes a confusion of personality.If the personality of an affiliated company is mixed and the interests of creditors are seriously damaged, the affiliated companies shall bear joint and several liabilities for external debts among themselves.In the first instance, the company recognized the fact that Yinyan Company collected part of the equity transfer funds on its behalf, and said "unclear" when answering the court of first instance's inquiry about "how to deal with the financial situation", and Yinyan Company did not submit evidence to prove that it has a contractual basis for collecting the relevant equity transfer funds on behalf of the company.Besides, Yinyan and Bavarian have not provided evidence to prove whether part of the money collected has been returned to Bavarian.As there is a transfer of interests without a contractual basis between the two companies, resulting in unclear property boundaries and financial confusion between the two companies, the court of first instance has ruled that it is not improper for Yinyan Company to bear joint and several liability for the debts of the company.

In the case of (2021) Supreme People's Court Min Zai No. 342, the court of second instance held that Jinyuan Trading Company, Huiye Trading Company, and Yongxing Instrument Factory appeared to be independent from each other on the surface, but all three companies (or factories) were controlled by Sun Heping or his immediate family members. Sun Heping or his immediate family members, as the controlling shareholders or actual controllers, controlled the three companies (or factories), abused their control rights, resulting in unclear property boundaries among the three companies (or factories), mutual transfer of interests, loss of independent personality, and becoming tools for evading debts, seriously damaging the interests of the creditors of Jinyuan Trading Company. Therefore, the judgment of first instance, on the grounds that Jinyuan Trading Company, the former Jinyuan Trading Company, Huiye Trading Company, and others had mixed personality and property, did not support the claims of Huiye Trading Company and Yongxing Instrument Factory to exclude the execution of the part of compensation for the demolition and relocation of collective land use rights and temporary buildings involved in the case, which had corresponding factual and legal basis. The judgment of second instance was wrong to modify the original judgment, and the Supreme People's Court corrected it.
2. Excessive Domination and Control

Once a company is abused by a shareholder, it will no longer have independent will and independent interests, and its independent personality will be reduced to props. If it still abides by the independent personality of the company, it will seriously damage the interests of the creditors of the company, and the company's personality should be denied at this time.Where multiple affiliated companies are controlled by the same person, husband and wife, mother and child or family, when determining whether the corporate personality should be negatived, the focus should be on whether there is any abuse of control.The following circumstances can generally be recognized as abuse of control: a subsidiary controlled by the same shareholder transfers benefits to other subsidiaries;When an affiliated company conducts transactions with other companies, the gains shall belong to one party of the affiliated company, but the losses shall be borne by the other party of the affiliated company;Withdraw the company's funds or dissolve the company first, and then set up another company with the original equipment, place, personnel and the same business purpose, so as to evade the debts of the original company.

blocks.content: In the case of (2019) Su Min Zhong No. 1528, regarding whether Guofa Energy Conservation Company, Guofa Logistics Company, Guo Liucheng, and Guofa China Enterprise Company had mixed personalities, and whether they should bear joint and several liability for the repayment of the debts involved in the case owed by Guofa China Enterprise Company to Guodian Photovoltaic Company, the court held that after trial, the court made clear determinations on the relationship and liability bearing among Guofa Energy Conservation Company, Guofa Logistics Company, Guo Liucheng, and Guofa China Enterprise Company. The specific results are as follows:

blocks.text: ①It is determined that Guofa Energy Conservation Company, Guofa Logistics Company, and Guofa China Enterprise Company have mixed personalities and shall bear joint and several liability for repayment.

There is confusion or cross-talk among Guofa Energy Conservation Company, Guofa Logistics Company and Guofa China Enterprise Company in terms of domicile, contact information, senior managers and business scope.According to the industrial and commercial information, recruitment information and annual report provided by Guodian Photovoltaic Company, the three companies are all located in Xicheng District, Beijing, with the same contact number, and the e-mail address of Guofa Logistics Company and Guofa Huaqi Company is the same.At the same time, Guo Liucheng, Han Xia and Wang Xueguan hold cross-posts in the three companies, and the business scope of the three companies involves machinery, electrical appliances and other related businesses.Although the relevant subject raises an objection, the court shall not accept its claim because it fails to provide valid evidence to prove it.

From the details of the five bank accounts of the Sino-foreign Development China Enterprise Company transferred by the court of first instance, it can be proved that there is financial confusion among the three companies.During 2011-2018, there was a large amount of capital exchanges between the three companies, and most of the purposes of funds were not clearly marked;The project collection and financial subsidies of Sino-foreign Enterprises are frequently and in large amount transferred out to Sino-foreign Energy Conservation Company and Sino-foreign Logistics Company;The funds transferred from the State Development Energy Conservation Company and the State Development Logistics Company to the State Development China Enterprise Company are basically equal to the daily operating expenses of the State Development China Enterprise Company.Guofa Energy Conservation Company, Guofa Logistics Company and Guo Liucheng refused to provide financial books to cooperate with the judicial audit, nor did they provide other rebuttal evidence. The court of first instance accepted the verification opinions of Guodian Photovoltaic Company on bank flow, and found that the financial confusion of the three companies was not improper.In the second instance, the relevant subject only denied the financial confusion on the grounds that the bank flow cannot fully reflect the flow of funds, and the court did not recognize it.

②It is determined that Guo Liucheng, as the actual controller, excessively dominated and controlled the companies, and shall bear joint and several liability for repayment: According to the facts ascertained in the first instance, Guo Liucheng, through the equity control chain, i.e., holding 97.83% of the equity of Guofa Logistics Company, Guofa Logistics Company holding 99.69% of the equity of Guofa Energy Conservation Company, and Guofa Energy Conservation Company holding 93.75% of the equity of Guofa China Enterprise Company, was able to actually control and dominate Guofa Energy Conservation Company and Guofa China Enterprise Company, and at the same time served as a personal shareholder of both companies and held senior management positions. In accordance with the provisions of paragraph 1 of Article 108 of the Interpretation of the Supreme People's Court on the Application of the Civil Procedure Law of the People's Republic of China, it is highly probable that Guo Liucheng is the actual controller of the three companies. Guofa Energy Conservation Company, Guofa Logistics Company, and Guo Liucheng appealed to deny but failed to provide rebuttal evidence, and shall bear adverse consequences. Guo Liucheng, as the actual controller, excessively dominated and controlled the companies, resulting in unclear property boundaries, financial confusion, and loss of independent personality among the three companies, leading to Guofa China Enterprise Company's inability to pay off the large debts owed to Guodian Photovoltaic Company, which seriously damaged the interests of the company's creditors. Therefore, he shall bear joint and several liability for the company's debts.

Therefore, the Court considers that the appeal requests of Guofa Energy Conservation Company, Guofa Logistics Company and Guo Liucheng lack factual and legal basis and shall not be supported.If the facts are clearly ascertained and the law is correctly applied in the judgment of first instance, the appeal shall be rejected and the original judgment shall be upheld.The application for retrial submitted by Guofa Energy Conservation and Environmental Protection Development Group Co., Ltd. to the Supreme People's Court was also rejected.
(3) Subjective elements

The shareholder has the intention to evade the debt.This element is usually judged in combination with the act of control and the act of abuse. In practice, under the condition of meeting other elements, the proof of this element can generally be completed without the creditor providing additional evidence.
(4) Consequence elements

The interests of the creditors of the company must be seriously damaged.The judgment standard is usually that the debtor is unable to pay off the due debts.If the creditor can prove that there is abuse, it can generally be presumed that the result requirement is met, and there is no need to prove that the debtor is insolvent.Moreover, the result element is not based on the premise that it cannot be remedied in other ways, and creditors have the right to choose to directly apply the horizontal penetration rule to safeguard their own rights and interests.
III. Key Points for Creditors to Safeguard Their Rights
(1) burden of proof
